programme.title: James Martin's Great British Adventure

programme.title: Mary Berry Everyday
programme.desc: The food writer and former Great British Bake Off judge celebrates the everyday food and ingredients she has always loved, from hearty and wholesome ideas to dishes that are a little bit more indulgent. Mary begins by travelling to Scotland to enjoy the best of the country's staple foods, preparing recipes inspired by her own Scots heritage. She discovers wonderful shellfish in Ullapool, joins chef Tom Kitchin to share the joy of venison cooked on a barbecue and whips up her special whisky cream on the banks of a loch

programme.title: Mary Berry Everyday
programme.desc: The food writer and former Great British Bake Off judge continues her celebration of the everyday food and ingredients she has always loved to cook, and which have formed the cornerstone of her work for more than six decades. In this edition, Mary shares recipes for inspiring hearty dishes full of wholesome goodness, from her secret tip for a rich bolognese sauce to a soup that will warm the cockles. She also dons a white suit and tries her hand at beekeeping, collecting fresh honey to make a winning pudding

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz show in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot.

programme.desc: Kirk sets Scotty up with a date to help him recover from a recent injury, but doesn't bargain on his friend becoming the centre of a murder investigation. Sci-fi adventure, starring William Shatner and James Doohan

programme.title: The Six Million Dollar Man
programme.desc: Plans to take the legendary Liberty Bell on a tour of America are thwarted by an unbalanced scientist threatening to destroy it

programme.title: T.J. Hooker
programme.desc: Former partner Jim Cody asks Hooker to help him crack a drug-related murder, only to find the case has unfortunate personal repercussions. William Shatner and Jim Brown star

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Four female inmate escape from a prison bus and two of them return to robbing banks. Meanwhile, Alex discovers one of the women had the wrong legal representation during her trial

programme.title: The Last Stand (2013)
programme.desc: Disgraced narcotics cop Ray Owens retires into a quiet life as sheriff of a small town on the Mexican border. He gets the chance to prove his worth as a law enforcer when a drug lord escapes from prison and tries to flee the US - with his escape route taking him through Ray's jurisdiction. Action thriller, starring Arnold Schwarzenegger, Johnny Knoxville and Forest Whitaker

programme.title: The Fan (1996)
programme.desc: An unbalanced baseball fan takes hero worship to extremes while attempting to help a player get over a recent slump - but it is not long before the sports star realises his new friend is seriously disturbed. Tony Scott's thriller, starring Robert De Niro, Wesley Snipes, Ellen Barkin and John Leguizamo

programme.title: Best of the Best
programme.desc: A martial arts team is assembled to represent America in an international taekwondo tournament in South Korea, but conflict within the squad threatens their chances of victory. Martial arts drama, starring Eric Roberts, James Earl Jones, Phillip Rhee and Sally Kirkland.

programme.title: POW: The Escape (1986)
programme.desc: As the Vietnam War comes to an end, one man embarks on a life-threatening mission to free American prisoners held in a secret Vietcong camp. Action, starring David Carradine

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Four female inmate escape from a prison bus and two of them return to robbing banks. Meanwhile, Alex discovers one of the women had the wrong legal representation during her trial

programme.title: T.J. Hooker
programme.desc: Former partner Jim Cody asks Hooker to help him crack a drug-related murder, only to find the case has unfortunate personal repercussions. William Shatner and Jim Brown star

programme.title: The Six Million Dollar Man
programme.desc: Plans to take the legendary Liberty Bell on a tour of America are thwarted by an unbalanced scientist threatening to destroy it

programme.title: Meteor Moon (2020)
programme.desc: A meteor crashes into the moon and shifts its axis, sending it on a collision course with Earth - leaving a team of scientists scratching their heads in a bid to prevent a global disaster. Sci-fi adventure, starring Dominique Swain, Michael Broderick and Chris Boudreaux

programme.title: Legend Lowdown
programme.desc: Take a look at all the latest movies and series coming your way this month

programme.title: MacShayne: Winner Takes All (1994)
programme.desc: Crime drama, starring Kenny Rogers as Las Vegas casino detective John J MacShayne, who finds himself confronted by artful tricksters and corrupt police commissioners. With Ann Jillian, Wendy Phillips and John Karlen

programme.title: Legend Lowdown
programme.desc: Take a look at all the latest movies and series coming your way this month

programme.title: MacShayne: Final Roll of the Dice (1994)
programme.desc: Kenny Rogers plays Las Vegas casino troubleshooter John MacShayne, who gets mixed up in the murder of a famous entertainer. Crime drama, with Maria Conchita Alonso, Michael McKean and Daniel Hugh Kelly

programme.title: Legend Lowdown
programme.desc: Take a look at all the latest movies and series coming your way this month

programme.title: Star Trek
programme.desc: The Federation's demand to allow an unusual cargo aboard leads to the Enterprise being overrun by small furry creatures which breed at an alarming rate and feast on a rare wheat grain. Sci-fi adventure, starring William Shatner and Leonard Nimoy

programme.title: The Six Million Dollar Man
programme.desc: Steve Austin investigates when a famous entertainer is suspected of working for an international spy ring. Sonny Bono guest stars

programme.title: T.J. Hooker
programme.desc: A recently released convict tries to exact revenge on Hooker, whom he blames for the death of his long-time cellmate. However, Romano gets caught in the crossfire, resulting in damage to his sight and his girlfriend getting killed. Vintage American drama, starring William Shatner and Adrian Zmed

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: The lawman organises basketball tournaments in a bid to stop gang related activities. Chuck Norris stars

programme.title: Cape Fear (1991)
programme.desc: A psychopathic rapist is released from prison after 14 years and plots revenge on the defence attorney he blames for his imprisonment. Subjecting the lawyer and his family to a terrifying campaign of intimidation, he uses his knowledge of the law to stay untouchable. Martin Scorsese's thriller remake, starring Robert De Niro, Nick Nolte, Jessica Lange and Juliette Lewis, with cameo appearances by Gregory Peck, Robert Mitchum and Martin Balsam, who all appeared in the 1962 original

programme.title: Bullet to the Head (2012)
programme.desc: Two hitmen are betrayed by their employer and one of them is killed, leaving the survivor swearing revenge. His search for the assassin leads him to a police detective investigating the murder of his partner, and they join forces when they realise one man is behind both deaths. Action thriller, starring Sylvester Stallone, Sung Kang and Christian Slater

programme.title: Vengeance: A Love Story (2017)
programme.desc: A single mother is gang raped while her daughter is forced to watch, but the perpetrators escape justice by attacking her character in court. A police officer who was called to the crime scene assembles a group of strangers to help the woman get vengeance on her attackers. Thriller, starring Nicolas Cage and Anna Hutchison

programme.title: Aftermath (2017)
programme.desc: A hard-working construction worker is forced to deal with life-shattering loss when his family are killed in a plane crash. Drama, starring Arnold Schwarzenegger, Scoot McNairy and Maggie Grace

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Twelve-year-old George is airlifted to St George's after he is struck by a motorcycle on his way home from school, and doctors are concerned about the extensive damage to his leg, which could lead to permanent disability. Meanwhile, 26-year-old Tanveer has come off his moped at speed, suffering a serious head injury that has left him with no memory of the event. In paediatrics, nine-year-old Amber is admitted, suffering severe stomach pains and vomiting, with doctors worried it could be appendicitis

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: The return of the documentary about life inside an A&E department, following patients treated in the same 24-hour period at St George's in south-west London. Fourteen-year-old Eduard is rushed in after falling from a swing and fracturing his ankle, while 62-year-old Rosina is treated having broken her wrist on the dancefloor at a family party. Paula is in Resus having been unable to take in food through her feeding tube and it is not only her health that is in danger, but also that of her unborn baby

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Forty-three-year-old Mohammad, who has motor neurone disease, is rushed to St George's with severe sepsis and doctors work to get the infection under control, while David has fallen 20 feet from a billboard and the trauma team is concerned that the 47-year-old has suffered life-changing injuries. Retired journalist Tom is in the urgent care centre after knocking his arm and his wife Eileen reveals how their plans have been turned upside down due to an unexpected bombshell six weeks previously

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: The paramedics treat a cafe worker struggling to speak, having possibly suffered a stroke. A woman who's in the process of giving birth to her seventh child hasn't felt the baby move in over 10 hours and there are signs the baby could be in distress. Another pregnant woman and her toddler son are involved in a car crash, and a patient is found collapsed in a car park

programme.title: The Perfect Pitch
programme.desc: This week's camping connoisseurs go in search of the perfect pitch in north Wales. Their first stop is the coastal campsite of Porth Iago on the Llyn Peninsula

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: In Sheffield, Henry Cole makes good money restoring an antique pine sideboard, but has less success with a pair of old brass jugs. And Simon O'Brien converts a staircase into shelves and makes an eye-watering profit from restoring an optician's desk

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien visit Hampshire and Surrey, where Henry makes a pair of coffee tables from a cable drum and a galvanised water tank, and Simon rescues a winged back chair and upholsters it with his own designer fabric

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The sports car-themed PitStop in Hertfordshire hosts the first round, as owners Melvyn and Sindy Rutter try to make a good impression. However, the absence of tea and coffee in the rooms does not go down well with the guests, and Melvyn's after-dinner talk about his collection of Morgan cars fails to spark much interest

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Gill and Peter Tydie invite their fellow competitors to stay at the Victory in Mersea, Essex, a four-star B&B with 26 members of staff to run the bar, restaurant and accommodation. A trip to a local fishery gives one contestant an opportunity to annoy his peers, and the discovery of a hole in a bedsheet leaves the hosts feeling uneasy about their chances of success

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Dave Botha hosts at his Butterfly Paragliding B&B on the Isle of Wight. Deborah Morrison and Wayne Bingham are unhappy they must sleep in a tepee in the garden and use an outdoor toilet, and claim they were served watery eggs and rubbery sausages for breakfast

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Deborah Morrison and Wayne Bingham host the final night at Braddon Hall in Torquay, Devon, where Melvyn and Sindy take issue with their squeaky four-poster bed. The hoteliers try to entertain their guests by inviting them to take part in an archery contest - which ends in accusations of unfair play

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time to discover how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers and to air their grievances. One of the hosts gets upset about large underpayments and a couple interrogate their guests about the criticism in their feedback, before the winner is announced

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the first night in Kent, 51-year-old construction site manager and part-time bouncer Mark hopes that his dinner party can be as much about the party as it is about the dinner, as he hosts a cheesy fun-fuelled evening. Will party poppers, balloons, confetti cannons and surprising celebrity stories impress 53-year-old dog-lover Karen, 39-year-old Czech shop assistant Verush, 42-year-old singer Amrick and 58-year-old fashion photographer Anthony?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the second night in Kent, competitive sales assistant Verush is going all out to win by hosting a Czech-themed evening, but ex-stripper Mark isn't impressed, comparing Verush's pasta to MDF and pastry to leather, while the first night's argument between dog lover Karen and photographer Anthony is reignited

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Dog enthusiast Karen hosts the third night of a week of dinner parties in Kent, hoping to impress her guests by serving up a menu inspired by her four-legged friends. After the cheesy fun of the previous night, she is determined to impress her guests

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Amrick takes his turn to host in Kent, hoping to wow his guests by revealing his musical talents and with his traditional Indian cuisine. But with dog-lover Karen and competitive Verush not liking hot and spicy food, there could be trouble - while photographer Anthony questions ex-stripper Mark's dress sense

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Photographer Anthony attempts to wow his guests with an evening of fine food and culinary excellence on the final day of the competition in Kent. However, the soiree is jeopardised by ex-stripper Mark on overdrive and dog-lover Karen showcasing some incredible impressions from animals to Anthony himself. The host might be cockily confident he can win, but Karen seemingly remains in pole position to bag the £1,000 prize

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contest begins at Swallows Rest in Weymouth, Dorset, where hosts Jane and Keith Smith are pulling out all the stops to win. However, the colour combinations in the rooms do not go down well with rival B&B owner Ray Chowdhury, and an afternoon of drawing starts some competition between Ray and newlyweds James and Darren Kosa-Jones

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants visit the Burley Inn in the New Forest, where newlyweds Darren and James welcome their guests. Donna and David prove to have a critical eye when they discover holes in the sheets, and Jane and Keith have a disagreement over a toilet roll. In the afternoon, the couple reveal their quizmaster skills, before Ray grills them on how they run their B&B over dinner

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to Hotel Montville in Redditch, Worcestershire for the third day of the competition, where first impressions of Ray Chowdhury's unique style and decor are positive. However, room inspections leave Jane and Keith Smith threatening not to stay, and the guests grill their host about his business background at dinner. Although some light relief is given in the form of ice sculpting, breakfast disappoints James and Darren Kosa-Jones, and Ray must face the harsh reality of his guests' opinions at feedback

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The last visit is to Donna and David's Silverton B&B in Shrewsbury, where the complimentary earplugs soon have Darren and James worrying about the noise from the road outside. A Segway race helps whip up the guests' competitive spirit, Donna gets emotional when her work-life balance is questioned, and chef David proves he can handle the unexpected at breakfast

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The owners meet for the last time to find out what they have been paid and settle some scores. Accusations fly around the table as payments are revealed and the winner announced. Swallows Rest is first up and hosts Jane and Keith want to find out about Ray's colour preferences. Next Darren and James of the Burley Inn quiz Ray over his fussiness around their pine furniture. When it comes to the Silverton, B&B team Donna and David take umbrage with Jane and Keith's comments

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp helps Jacinta and Marco, who have outgrown their home in Didsbury, Greater Manchester, while Phil Spencer assists newly engaged Olivia and Amy, who want to settle back in the North after five years on the move

programme.title: PopMaster TV
programme.desc: Ken Bruce hosts the fourth heat. Relive the hits and see if you really know the charts! Only one PopMaster player can secure their place to play 3 in 10 - will they leave with a silver or gold disc?

programme.title: Kevin McCloud's Listed Britain
programme.desc: Kevin looks at bold, modern places that challenge the idea of what heritage can be, and the places that can be the easiest to dismiss and the hardest to save. He steps inside Liverpool's Metropolitan Cathedral, which is flooded with coloured light from one of Britain's most spectacular stained-glass lanterns, and visits London Zoo's penguin pool, where the aquatic birds are treated to cutting-edge modernism

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Seventy-year-old lung cancer patient Marion is rushed to St George's with suspected sepsis. She was discharged from hospital the previous day after having an operation to remove liquid from her lung, but has deteriorated overnight. Eighty-three-year-old Roy is brought into resus after falling off his roof at home, and doctors are concerned that he may have sustained internal injuries, while, five-year-old Laila has come to paediatrics with her mum Clarissa after cutting her head open at school

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A look at the night shift in a busy A&E department. A woman with a severe form of autism is brought in, having suffered an injury to one of her fingers. While she is treated, her sister shares stories of their childhood and the challenges their family have faced. An 82-year-old patient has difficulty breathing, and doctors suspect he has developed life-threatening sepsis and need to act fast to try to halt the spread of infection

programme.title: Kevin McCloud's Listed Britain
programme.desc: Kevin looks at bold, modern places that challenge the idea of what heritage can be, and the places that can be the easiest to dismiss and the hardest to save. He steps inside Liverpool's Metropolitan Cathedral, which is flooded with coloured light from one of Britain's most spectacular stained-glass lanterns, and visits London Zoo's penguin pool, where the aquatic birds are treated to cutting-edge modernism

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Seventy-year-old lung cancer patient Marion is rushed to St George's with suspected sepsis. She was discharged from hospital the previous day after having an operation to remove liquid from her lung, but has deteriorated overnight. Eighty-three-year-old Roy is brought into resus after falling off his roof at home, and doctors are concerned that he may have sustained internal injuries, while, five-year-old Laila has come to paediatrics with her mum Clarissa after cutting her head open at school

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Twelve-year-old George is airlifted to St George's after he is struck by a motorcycle on his way home from school, and doctors are concerned about the extensive damage to his leg, which could lead to permanent disability. Meanwhile, 26-year-old Tanveer has come off his moped at speed, suffering a serious head injury that has left him with no memory of the event. In paediatrics, nine-year-old Amber is admitted, suffering severe stomach pains and vomiting, with doctors worried it could be appendicitis

programme.title: The Perfect Pitch
programme.desc: The camping connoisseurs arrive at Cefn Coed, nestled within an ancient oak forest near the Mawddach Estuary, where highlights include forest bathing and a Thai cooking class

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien head to Redditch where Henry restores a 1970s Kawasaki motorbike, while Simon takes a pair of rusty cinema seat supports and creates a desirable garden bench

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien visit Wales, where Henry restores a Royal Enfield motorbike, converting it into a classic cafe racer. Simon's choice of an old school house radiator springs a leak, but he gets an 1980s pinball machine back to its best

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Simon White and Shirley Boyle, owners of the Old Hall Country House in Madeley, Cheshire, host the week's first round of the B&B competition. The guests are impressed by the 16th-century building, and are taken tap-dancing and to a private fine-dining restaurant, but one of them is dissatisfied with her bedroom's layout and the garnish on her eggs at breakfast

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Eileen Finn invites her fellow contestants to the four-star Thornley House in Allendale, Northumberland, for the second round of the contest, but struggles to follow the impressive first event. The hostess's pet cats are not to all of her guests' liking, and with activities including a tour of a feline cemetery, the theme for the stay is made abundantly clear

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Jessica and Adrian Rashleigh are the third hosts, at their three-bedroom establishment Chy Conyn in St Ives, Cornwall. For the activity they arrange a magical mystery camper van tour of the local sights. Some of the guests struggle with the small breakfast room but the food is a success, and Eileen Finn demonstrates her karate skills on the men

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final stay is at the Cavendish in Bournemouth, run by Tim and Karen Sturgess. On arrival, the competitors are impressed by the 'pink palace', but things go downhill when a bed is broken and one couple are allocated a room next to the front door. However, Eileen is delighted by the feline memorabilia in her room and the group enjoys a spot of indoor paint-balling and an open-top bus tour of the resort

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time to discover how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers and to air their grievances. Simon White of Old Hall Country House is surprised by an overpayment and Eileen Finn from the four-star Thornley receives tough criticism, before the winner is announced

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The first host in Devon, 35-year-old salon owner Hannah, hopes to impress her guests with a menu bursting with taste sensations. But Hannah's technique of combining her favourite flavours may not tickle all of her foodie guests' taste buds. Hannah's night is filled with laughter and revelations, though there is an inkling around that her food is not tasty enough

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On day two of the competition in Devon, funeral director and BMX-fanatic Simon plans an evening to get his guests' hearts racing, with some so-called 'man' food and a display by one of the UK's leading BMX riders to get their adrenaline pumping. Such innovation, he hopes, will bag him the cash prize at the end of the week

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Body artist Pascale hosts the third night of a week of dinner parties in Devon, preparing a Vietnamese menu full of flavour and colour in an effort to impress her guests enough to win the £1,000 prize money. However, her cooking attire for the starter leaves little to the imagination, Nick and Simon find some unusual artwork in her bedroom, Hannah discovers an unwanted guest in her main course, and Nick hits a nerve with Ajay

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Nick hosts the fourth night in Devon, preparing a menu that he hopes represents his personality - big, bold and brash - although after his argument with Ajay, he is planning on turning down the banter. However, salon owner Hannah is not impressed with Nick's venison main and ladybird-loving Pascale finds the portions a bit too big. But undertaker Simon is happy to polish off any leftovers - is Nick in with a chance of bagging the £1,000 prize?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Estate agent Ajay hopes her nautical-themed evening will see her sail to victory on the fifth and final night in Devon. Along with a gigantic seafood spread, she has also laid on a boat trip around Torquay harbour. However, all does not go quite to plan in the galley. The guests later gather to discover who has won the £1,000 prize

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contest begins at the King's Head in Addlethorpe, Lincolnshire, where landlady Helen Coy and her bar manager and best friend Emma Green are pulling out all the stops to win. The B&B owners are taken back to their childhood at the funfair, At dinner, B&B beginners Louise and Andy Macbeth rattle nerves when Louise reveals her past career as a hospitality journalist - leaving pub veteran Helen less than impressed

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: B&B newcomers Andy and Louise Macbeth invite their rival hoteliers to spend the night at the dog-friendly Beck Hall in North Yorkshire, where an extra-critical Helen and Emma discover what they believe to be stray dog hairs in their bed. A fishing trip makes Terri feel a little out of sorts, and when the time arrives for feedback, the managers are surprised to learn two of their guests have indicated they would not come back for a second stay

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to the Albaston in Torquay for the third day of the competition, where they are welcomed by owners and avid souvenir collectors, Kevin and Julie Shadbolt. Terri and Stuart are impressed by their room, but are startled by the memorabilia in the communal lounge, and expectations are raised at dinner when Kevin reveals he has a secret recipe for success when it comes to breakfast. However, at feedback the next day, tensions begin to rise

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final stay of the week is in Bournemouth, at Terri and Stewart's specialist dance holiday venue the Hotel Piccadilly, where a class in the grand ballroom soon has the guests in a spin. However, temperatures rise over dinner when the simmering tension between Louise and Helen finally boils over

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The owners meet up for the last time to find out what they've been paid and to settle some scores. King's Head landlady Helen challenges Louise and Andy about the problems they reported on cleanliness in the bathroom. while Louise and Andy from Beck Hall get hot under the collar when the others criticise their dog-friendly approach

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer help two sets of house-hunters looking for homes in Birmingham. Kirstie helps sewing blogger Elle and her fiance Ed, who have seen 52 properties and still cannot find the perfect pad. Phil helps Ben and Laura, who are relocating from Bristol. Their main concern is that they do not get stuck in suburbia

programme.title: PopMaster TV
programme.desc: Ken Bruce hosts the fifth heat as he tests more music lovers from across the UK, who are all hoping to give a smash hit performance and book their spot in the grand final

programme.title: Great British Train Journeys from Above
programme.desc: Hugh Bonneville narrates a journey through the rolling hills of England's south-west on the West Somerset Railway created by Isambard Kingdom Brunel. Widely recognised as one of the greatest engineers of all time, Brunel's remarkable railways and structures are known worldwide. This episode travels along his railway through the Quantock Hills to the Bristol Channel

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Junior doctor Silas works his first ever shift in resus, and is part of the team brought in to treat a biker who is rushed to A&E after riding into a lamppost. A two-year-old boy is treated for injuries resulting from a fall, and his mother shares the story of how her eldest son was recently attacked by a gang

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: This edition follows the stories of people who have lived through tragedy and life-changing circumstances, exploring how they've overcome their loss and adapted to the new paths their lives have taken. Steve, 52, is rushed to St George's after falling over and hitting his head on a concrete step, while 21-year-old Fleur is assessed after she woke up with pain and swelling in her leg. Muffy, 72, is brought to Resus with slurred speech and weakness in her legs, and doctors suspect she may have suffered a stroke

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: A two-year-old is having a constant seizure and needs to get to hospital urgently; he won't stop fitting and his oxygen levels are falling. A young woman is involved in a road traffic collision with a lorry. A paramedic opens up to a patient about losing a loved one to cancer, and a 91-year-old woman with chest pain and a history of severe heart complaints seeks assistance by calling 999

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the first night in Kent, 51-year-old construction site manager and part-time bouncer Mark hopes that his dinner party can be as much about the party as it is about the dinner, as he hosts a cheesy fun-fuelled evening. Will party poppers, balloons, confetti cannons and surprising celebrity stories impress 53-year-old dog-lover Karen, 39-year-old Czech shop assistant Verush, 42-year-old singer Amrick and 58-year-old fashion photographer Anthony?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the second night in Kent, competitive sales assistant Verush is going all out to win by hosting a Czech-themed evening, but ex-stripper Mark isn't impressed, comparing Verush's pasta to MDF and pastry to leather, while the first night's argument between dog lover Karen and photographer Anthony is reignited

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Dog enthusiast Karen hosts the third night of a week of dinner parties in Kent, hoping to impress her guests by serving up a menu inspired by her four-legged friends. After the cheesy fun of the previous night, she is determined to impress her guests

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Amrick takes his turn to host in Kent, hoping to wow his guests by revealing his musical talents and with his traditional Indian cuisine. But with dog-lover Karen and competitive Verush not liking hot and spicy food, there could be trouble - while photographer Anthony questions ex-stripper Mark's dress sense

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Photographer Anthony attempts to wow his guests with an evening of fine food and culinary excellence on the final day of the competition in Kent. However, the soiree is jeopardised by ex-stripper Mark on overdrive and dog-lover Karen showcasing some incredible impressions from animals to Anthony himself. The host might be cockily confident he can win, but Karen seemingly remains in pole position to bag the £1,000 prize

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contest begins at Swallows Rest in Weymouth, Dorset, where hosts Jane and Keith Smith are pulling out all the stops to win. However, the colour combinations in the rooms do not go down well with rival B&B owner Ray Chowdhury, and an afternoon of drawing starts some competition between Ray and newlyweds James and Darren Kosa-Jones

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants visit the Burley Inn in the New Forest, where newlyweds Darren and James welcome their guests. Donna and David prove to have a critical eye when they discover holes in the sheets, and Jane and Keith have a disagreement over a toilet roll. In the afternoon, the couple reveal their quizmaster skills, before Ray grills them on how they run their B&B over dinner

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to Hotel Montville in Redditch, Worcestershire for the third day of the competition, where first impressions of Ray Chowdhury's unique style and decor are positive. However, room inspections leave Jane and Keith Smith threatening not to stay, and the guests grill their host about his business background at dinner. Although some light relief is given in the form of ice sculpting, breakfast disappoints James and Darren Kosa-Jones, and Ray must face the harsh reality of his guests' opinions at feedback

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The last visit is to Donna and David's Silverton B&B in Shrewsbury, where the complimentary earplugs soon have Darren and James worrying about the noise from the road outside. A Segway race helps whip up the guests' competitive spirit, Donna gets emotional when her work-life balance is questioned, and chef David proves he can handle the unexpected at breakfast

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The owners meet for the last time to find out what they have been paid and settle some scores. Accusations fly around the table as payments are revealed and the winner announced. Swallows Rest is first up and hosts Jane and Keith want to find out about Ray's colour preferences. Next Darren and James of the Burley Inn quiz Ray over his fussiness around their pine furniture. When it comes to the Silverton, B&B team Donna and David take umbrage with Jane and Keith's comments

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp helps Jacinta and Marco, who have outgrown their home in Didsbury, Greater Manchester, while Phil Spencer assists newly engaged Olivia and Amy, who want to settle back in the North after five years on the move

programme.title: PopMaster TV
programme.desc: Ken Bruce hosts the fourth heat. Relive the hits and see if you really know the charts! Only one PopMaster player can secure their place to play 3 in 10 - will they leave with a silver or gold disc?

programme.title: Kevin McCloud's Listed Britain
programme.desc: Kevin looks at bold, modern places that challenge the idea of what heritage can be, and the places that can be the easiest to dismiss and the hardest to save. He steps inside Liverpool's Metropolitan Cathedral, which is flooded with coloured light from one of Britain's most spectacular stained-glass lanterns, and visits London Zoo's penguin pool, where the aquatic birds are treated to cutting-edge modernism

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Seventy-year-old lung cancer patient Marion is rushed to St George's with suspected sepsis. She was discharged from hospital the previous day after having an operation to remove liquid from her lung, but has deteriorated overnight. Eighty-three-year-old Roy is brought into resus after falling off his roof at home, and doctors are concerned that he may have sustained internal injuries, while, five-year-old Laila has come to paediatrics with her mum Clarissa after cutting her head open at school

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Twelve-year-old George is airlifted to St George's after he is struck by a motorcycle on his way home from school, and doctors are concerned about the extensive damage to his leg, which could lead to permanent disability. Meanwhile, 26-year-old Tanveer has come off his moped at speed, suffering a serious head injury that has left him with no memory of the event. In paediatrics, nine-year-old Amber is admitted, suffering severe stomach pains and vomiting, with doctors worried it could be appendicitis

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: A two-year-old is having a constant seizure and needs to get to hospital urgently; he won't stop fitting and his oxygen levels are falling. A young woman is involved in a road traffic collision with a lorry. A paramedic opens up to a patient about losing a loved one to cancer, and a 91-year-old woman with chest pain and a history of severe heart complaints seeks assistance by calling 999

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E

programme.title: The Perfect Pitch
programme.desc: The camping connoisseurs arrive at Cefn Coed, nestled within an ancient oak forest near the Mawddach Estuary, where highlights include forest bathing and a Thai cooking class

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien head to Redditch where Henry restores a 1970s Kawasaki motorbike, while Simon takes a pair of rusty cinema seat supports and creates a desirable garden bench

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien visit Wales, where Henry restores a Royal Enfield motorbike, converting it into a classic cafe racer. Simon's choice of an old school house radiator springs a leak, but he gets an 1980s pinball machine back to its best

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Simon White and Shirley Boyle, owners of the Old Hall Country House in Madeley, Cheshire, host the week's first round of the B&B competition. The guests are impressed by the 16th-century building, and are taken tap-dancing and to a private fine-dining restaurant, but one of them is dissatisfied with her bedroom's layout and the garnish on her eggs at breakfast

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Eileen Finn invites her fellow contestants to the four-star Thornley House in Allendale, Northumberland, for the second round of the contest, but struggles to follow the impressive first event. The hostess's pet cats are not to all of her guests' liking, and with activities including a tour of a feline cemetery, the theme for the stay is made abundantly clear

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Jessica and Adrian Rashleigh are the third hosts, at their three-bedroom establishment Chy Conyn in St Ives, Cornwall. For the activity they arrange a magical mystery camper van tour of the local sights. Some of the guests struggle with the small breakfast room but the food is a success, and Eileen Finn demonstrates her karate skills on the men

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final stay is at the Cavendish in Bournemouth, run by Tim and Karen Sturgess. On arrival, the competitors are impressed by the 'pink palace', but things go downhill when a bed is broken and one couple are allocated a room next to the front door. However, Eileen is delighted by the feline memorabilia in her room and the group enjoys a spot of indoor paint-balling and an open-top bus tour of the resort

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time to discover how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers and to air their grievances. Simon White of Old Hall Country House is surprised by an overpayment and Eileen Finn from the four-star Thornley receives tough criticism, before the winner is announced

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The first host in Devon, 35-year-old salon owner Hannah, hopes to impress her guests with a menu bursting with taste sensations. But Hannah's technique of combining her favourite flavours may not tickle all of her foodie guests' taste buds. Hannah's night is filled with laughter and revelations, though there is an inkling around that her food is not tasty enough

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On day two of the competition in Devon, funeral director and BMX-fanatic Simon plans an evening to get his guests' hearts racing, with some so-called 'man' food and a display by one of the UK's leading BMX riders to get their adrenaline pumping. Such innovation, he hopes, will bag him the cash prize at the end of the week

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Body artist Pascale hosts the third night of a week of dinner parties in Devon, preparing a Vietnamese menu full of flavour and colour in an effort to impress her guests enough to win the £1,000 prize money. However, her cooking attire for the starter leaves little to the imagination, Nick and Simon find some unusual artwork in her bedroom, Hannah discovers an unwanted guest in her main course, and Nick hits a nerve with Ajay

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Nick hosts the fourth night in Devon, preparing a menu that he hopes represents his personality - big, bold and brash - although after his argument with Ajay, he is planning on turning down the banter. However, salon owner Hannah is not impressed with Nick's venison main and ladybird-loving Pascale finds the portions a bit too big. But undertaker Simon is happy to polish off any leftovers - is Nick in with a chance of bagging the £1,000 prize?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Estate agent Ajay hopes her nautical-themed evening will see her sail to victory on the fifth and final night in Devon. Along with a gigantic seafood spread, she has also laid on a boat trip around Torquay harbour. However, all does not go quite to plan in the galley. The guests later gather to discover who has won the £1,000 prize

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contest begins at the King's Head in Addlethorpe, Lincolnshire, where landlady Helen Coy and her bar manager and best friend Emma Green are pulling out all the stops to win. The B&B owners are taken back to their childhood at the funfair, At dinner, B&B beginners Louise and Andy Macbeth rattle nerves when Louise reveals her past career as a hospitality journalist - leaving pub veteran Helen less than impressed

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: B&B newcomers Andy and Louise Macbeth invite their rival hoteliers to spend the night at the dog-friendly Beck Hall in North Yorkshire, where an extra-critical Helen and Emma discover what they believe to be stray dog hairs in their bed. A fishing trip makes Terri feel a little out of sorts, and when the time arrives for feedback, the managers are surprised to learn two of their guests have indicated they would not come back for a second stay

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to the Albaston in Torquay for the third day of the competition, where they are welcomed by owners and avid souvenir collectors, Kevin and Julie Shadbolt. Terri and Stuart are impressed by their room, but are startled by the memorabilia in the communal lounge, and expectations are raised at dinner when Kevin reveals he has a secret recipe for success when it comes to breakfast. However, at feedback the next day, tensions begin to rise

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final stay of the week is in Bournemouth, at Terri and Stewart's specialist dance holiday venue the Hotel Piccadilly, where a class in the grand ballroom soon has the guests in a spin. However, temperatures rise over dinner when the simmering tension between Louise and Helen finally boils over

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The owners meet up for the last time to find out what they've been paid and to settle some scores. programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer help two sets of house-hunters looking for homes in Birmingham. programme.title: Great British Train Journeys from Above
programme.desc: Hugh Bonneville narrates a journey through the rolling hills of England's south-west on the West Somerset Railway created by Isambard Kingdom Brunel. Widely recognised as one of the greatest engineers of all time, Brunel's remarkable railways and structures are known worldwide. This episode travels along his railway through the Quantock Hills to the Bristol Channel

programme.title: Murder on CCTV
programme.desc: In Galveston, Texas, the bodies of two women lie behind a grocery store. Police turn to surveillance footage, hoping to discover who killed them - and why

programme.title: The Killer in My Family
programme.desc: Ian Huntley's ex-girlfriend and daughter tell their side of the story. The pair explain how Huntley abused them for years, before they eventually ended all contact with him

programme.title: American Monster
programme.desc: On the night of her son's wedding, Christy is found dead. As they investigate, police uncover a dangerous marriage and a violent monster

programme.title: American Monster
programme.desc: After years of shooting practice, a West Virginia beauty takes her hobby too far after her ex-husband allows her back into his home

programme.title: American Detective with Lt Joe Kenda
programme.desc: In California, police find an accomplished U.S. Forest Service worker beneath a mound of snow. A multi-state manhunt for a deranged killer ensues.

programme.title: Judge Rinder's Crime Stories
programme.desc: The case of Julie Archer, who was set on fire in her home, and another involving the theft of 11 canisters of nitrous oxide, otherwise known as laughing gas, from a hospital in Darlington

programme.title: Murder on CCTV
programme.desc: The case of University of Florida student Christian Aguilar, who vanished from his campus in September 2012. Police were stumped until CCTV footage took the case in a shocking new direction, helping lead them to the 18-year-old's body

programme.title: Lethally Blonde
programme.desc: In 2007, a devastating house fire in Dallas Township, Pennsylvania, reveals the nearly decapitated body of a pornographic film director

programme.title: Evil Lives Here
programme.desc: After 30 years in an abusive marriage, Roxie finally leaves her husband. But he makes her pay a terrible price for that decision

programme.title: Murder on CCTV
programme.desc: When a loving couple fail to reach their holiday campsite, New Mexico State Police investigate. With no forensic evidence or witnesses, they scour CCTV footage for clues

programme.title: Murder on CCTV
programme.desc: Detectives scrutinise the case of 18-year-old Kyle Farishian, who was working a late-night shift at 7-Eleven when a masked assailant attacked him

programme.title: Forensic Detectives
programme.desc: A forensic anthropologist identifies human remains found in the Mississippi River, and a professor uses a combination of bones and mathematics to solve a murder

programme.title: Murder on CCTV
programme.desc: In 2013, a shopkeeper was shot dead in his store in Columbus, Ohio. With the shooting caught on camera and the killers on the run, detectives rushed to catch them

programme.title: Murder on CCTV
programme.desc: In 2014, eight-year-old Hiawayi Robinson left her apartment to play but never returned. Surveillance footage at a store led detectives to shocking discoveries

programme.title: The Yorkshire Auction House

programme.title: The Yorkshire Auction House
programme.sub-title: Toying with a New Life Down-Under
programme.desc: Angus unearths a treasure trove of toys in Manchester. Plus, three generations of heirlooms go under the gavel, but is their owner ready to sell?

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Peter Shahbenderian hopes wood expert Will Kirk can restore a pair of ornate Turkish bath clogs to their former glory. They were brought to the UK by Peter's mother when her family left Constantinople at the time of the Armenian genocide. Silversmith Brenton West is presented with two Isle of Man Manx Grand Prix trophies by Barrie James, which represent one of the greatest achievement of his father's racing days. Sadly over the years the trophies have become tarnished and broken. Presented by Jay Blades

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: James Braxton and Izzie Balmer hunt for profit-making antiques in south Wales. Jewellery specialist Izzie strikes gold while shopping, finding a bangle, pendant and earrings, and takes a punt on a Wedgwood pie dish. She also dips into the history of Tenby, a seaside town once a Georgian spa resort. Meanwhile, James accumulates a hoard of shiny items and one piece of silver causes a shock at auction. Later, the pair watch on from the impressive Cilwendeg Mansion in Pembrokeshire as the final auction of the trip takes place in Buckinghamshire

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Catherine Southon and Paul Laidlaw set out on a road trip through Northern Ireland to seek out bargains they hope will make a profit, with the first auction of the week being held in Omagh, Co Tyrone

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell help more people to reunite with long-lost relatives. Martin Smith grew up in Rochdale and had a happy childhood, but struggled with intense feelings of rejection when he discovered he was adopted and only now feels ready to face his fears and find his birth mother. Anne Jordan always knew she was adopted and has been searching for her birth mother for over 30 years, but she will not set a date for her wedding to fiance Chris until she has found her

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell tell more stories of people desperate to find missing family and answer questions that have haunted entire lives, beginning with two men searching for the truth about their beginnings. Paul Connolly had a brutal childhood and was put out with the rubbish as a baby in east London and raised in care, while Shaun Lawrence had a happy adoption with wonderful parents but was always curious about his birth parents

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell meet Sara Hathaway who, after losing her adoptive mother and older brother in the space of just a year, is desperate to find her birth family. programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell report on two more cases. More than 40 years ago, Caroline Kinsey was pregnant, alone and unable to provide the kind of family and upbringing she believed all children deserved. Burdened by her heart-breaking decision to give her son up for adoption, Caroline longs to find him, to hear that he did have a happy childhood, and to know whether he can forgive her after all these years. Raised as an only child, Paul McFarlane's beloved mother died when he was nine, but years later, a single piece of paper threw all his assumptions about his small family into question

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo sets off on the first leg of a trip from the Firth of Clyde to Lewis and Harris in the Outer Hebrides, beginning with a visit to a nuclear power station on the Hunterston Peninsula. He then learns about Largs' Viking past and admires one of the most beautiful railway stations in Britain at Wemyss Bay. The importance of ferries to connect western Scotland with its many islands becomes plain to Michael at Port Glasgow in the deep waters of the Clyde, where he visits Ferguson Marine, builders of Calmac ferries

programme.desc: The latest national and international news

programme.title: Uncharted with Ray Goggins
programme.desc: Singer Keith Duffy is venturing to Uganda with his actor son Jay for the test of a lifetime. Father and son will attempt to summit one of Africa's highest and least climbed peaks in the Rwenzori Mountains alongside Ray Goggins. It's a long and gruelling ascent of seven days through knee deep bog and alpine rock to its glacier peak, making it one of the continents toughest expeditions

programme.title: Maigret
programme.desc: As Maigret reels from regret, he suffers a blow in his personal life. Maigret digs into Fumal's life in order to understand him better

programme.title: Swindlers
programme.sub-title: Custom House Capital: A House of Cards
programme.desc: The Custom House Capital scandal, one of Ireland's most notorious investment frauds, which involved the systematic misappropriation of approximately 61 million euros in client funds. The fallout left more than 2,000 investors, many of whom were retirees, facing devastating financial losses. This documentary allows many of them to tell their stories about how their trust was breached, leaving them facing retirement with just a fraction of their life savings

programme.title: Tommy Sands: Clann an Cheoil
programme.desc: Documentary insight into one of Ireland's most successful and influential folk singer-songwriters, and how he and his family have acquired an international legacy as musicians, social activists and storytellers. Tommy and his family, Anne, Colum and Ben, have always been immersed in music and storytelling, their home a haven for Catholics and Protestants to come together to share tunes and tales. The film takes viewers back to where it all started and hears from those who have been influenced by the Sands family, including Arlo Gurhrie

programme.title: Welcome To Moore Street
programme.desc: Featuring a glimpse into the world of delivery riders in the Brazilian bike shop and learning about plans to restore the National Monument on the street. Young drag queen Noah performs on Moore Street during the Pride festival, while Georgian seamstress Liza celebrates one year in business

programme.title: Build Your Own Home
programme.desc: Young couple Sean and Jess O'Tuama from County Limerick are restoring a parochial house in rural County Limerick to its former glory. They plan to do some major structural repairs, tear apart the inside, reveal the original stonework and design their own kitchen and bedrooms. When the couple start peeling back layers of old plasterwork they realise that they have more work on their hands than they envisaged

programme.title: Kerry: Tides of Time
programme.desc: Part two of two. The story of a single year, as the seasons play out through and across the county's varied habitats. This programme looks in detail at the lives of some of the area's most charismatic and fascinating wild inhabitants. Badgers, red deer, hen harrier and puffins are among the cast of characters whose lives are followed through the year

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Though from Essex, Olly Murs is actually a quarter-Latvian. His late grandfather Eddie was from Latvia, but Olly knows very little about this side of the family and wants to find out more. The singer travels to the circus in Riga, where his great-grandfather Eduard worked. Olly meets the circus's archivist Elvira Avota, who shows him documents relating to his great-grandfather and his wife Katrine and their extraordinary double act. At the Latvian State Archives, historian Daina Bleiere shows him documents that relate to his great-grandmother. Olly discovers that in 1951 Veronika was put on trial, accused of making 'anti-Soviet' statements

programme.title: Feast of Love (2007)

Dick Van Dyke stars as the medical sleuth

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo sets off on the first leg of a trip from the Firth of Clyde to Lewis and Harris in the Outer Hebrides, beginning with a visit to a nuclear power station on the Hunterston Peninsula. He then learns about Largs' Viking past and admires one of the most beautiful railway stations in Britain at Wemyss Bay. The importance of ferries to connect western Scotland with its many islands becomes plain to Michael at Port Glasgow in the deep waters of the Clyde, where he visits Ferguson Marine, builders of Calmac ferries

programme.title: Neven's English Food Tour
programme.desc: Neven Maguire travels through the North East of England, visiting the region's culinary gems, tasting a variety of different dishes, and meeting the chefs behind them. programme.title: Uncharted with Ray Goggins
programme.desc: Singer Keith Duffy is venturing to Uganda with his actor son Jay for the test of a lifetime. Father and son will attempt to summit one of Africa's highest and least climbed peaks in the Rwenzori Mountains alongside Ray Goggins. It's a long and gruelling ascent of seven days through knee deep bog and alpine rock to its glacier peak, making it one of the continents toughest expeditions

programme.title: Maigret
programme.desc: As Maigret reels from regret, he suffers a blow in his personal life. Maigret digs into Fumal's life in order to understand him better

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Trixie cares for Arlean Brewer, a heavily pregnant unmarried woman recently discharged from the Linchmere Mental Hospital after being diagnosed with depression and mania. When Arlene struggles with her new psychiatric drug regime, Trixie must draw on all her medical and emotional skills to avert disaster. Rising rates of gonorrhoea in the district cause alarm, and Dr Turner decides to implement a contact-tracing scheme run by the Greater London Council. Rosalind and Cyril volunteer at a homeless shelter

programme.title: The Jetty
programme.desc: Ember is forced to confess to her family that human bone fragments were found in the boat hut, but Hannah can't believe her dad was responsible and pleads with Ember to look further. When a new development propels Ember into dangerous territory, the truth is finally revealed, and it proves far more shocking than she ever expected

programme.title: Tommy Sands: Clann an Cheoil
programme.desc: Documentary insight into one of Ireland's most successful and influential folk singer-songwriters, and how he and his family have acquired an international legacy as musicians, social activists and storytellers. Tommy and his family, Anne, Colum and Ben, have always been immersed in music and storytelling, their home a haven for Catholics and Protestants to come together to share tunes and tales. The film takes viewers back to where it all started and hears from those who have been influenced by the Sands family, including Arlo Gurhrie

programme.title: Welcome To Moore Street
programme.desc: Featuring a glimpse into the world of delivery riders in the Brazilian bike shop and learning about plans to restore the National Monument on the street. programme.title: Build Your Own Home
programme.desc: Young couple Sean and Jess O'Tuama from County Limerick are restoring a parochial house in rural County Limerick to its former glory. They plan to do some major structural repairs, tear apart the inside, reveal the original stonework and design their own kitchen and bedrooms. When the couple start peeling back layers of old plasterwork they realise that they have more work on their hands than they envisaged

programme.title: Kerry: Tides of Time

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Though from Essex, Olly Murs is actually a quarter-Latvian. His late grandfather Eddie was from Latvia, but Olly knows very little about this side of the family and wants to find out more. The singer travels to the circus in Riga, where his great-grandfather Eduard worked. Olly meets the circus's archivist Elvira Avota, who shows him documents relating to his great-grandfather and his wife Katrine and their extraordinary double act. At the Latvian State Archives, historian Daina Bleiere shows him documents that relate to his great-grandmother. Olly discovers that in 1951 Veronika was put on trial, accused of making 'anti-Soviet' statements

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud meets retired racehorse breeder John and his wife Helen in Dunstable. The couple met at Newbury races in 2005 and wed two years later, living in a 17th-century farmhouse until illness struck in 2018. Helen had a stroke, severely affecting her mobility and leaving her with aphasia, which affects her ability to communicate. With his wife constrained by the steep stairs, narrow doorways and changes in level of their current home, John resolved to make a big change by purchasing a nearby two-acre plot. Following the demolition of an old, dilapidated house on site, a new hi-tech accessible pavilion, complete with wildlife garden, will be built in its place for £1.3million

programme.desc: A thief takes a bank manager hostage while he and his gang make their getaway, only to later learn she lives in the same neighbourhood as him. Drama, directed by and starring Ben Affleck. With Rebecca Hall and Jeremy Renner

programme.title: Conversations with Friends
programme.desc: Frances is told the results of her abdominal scan. The diagnosis unsettles her - and she decides to keep the news to herself. The next morning Bobbi reveals that Melissa has shown her Frances's short story. Bobbi is furious with both the content of the story and Frances' dishonesty. Hurt by Frances too many times, she ends the friendship. Drama, starring Alison Oliver, Joe Alwyn, Sasha Lane and Jemima Kirke

programme.desc: Documentary about the life and career of Irish Times journalist Fintan O'Toole, in the year of his official retirement from the paper. The film follows the writer as he recalls his early life in Crumlin, south Dublin, setting the stage for his deep connection to the socio-political landscape of Ireland. It also delves into key events that have shaped contemporary Irish society, from a colonial legacy and the recent economic crises to dual Irish identity

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud meets retired racehorse breeder John and his wife Helen in Dunstable. The couple met at Newbury races in 2005 and wed two years later, living in a 17th-century farmhouse until illness struck in 2018. Helen had a stroke, severely affecting her mobility and leaving her with aphasia, which affects her ability to communicate. With his wife constrained by the steep stairs, narrow doorways and changes in level of their current home, John resolved to make a big change by purchasing a nearby two-acre plot. Following the demolition of an old, dilapidated house on site, a new hi-tech accessible pavilion, complete with wildlife garden, will be built in its place for £1.3million

programme.desc: While feeling lonely and low, Frances receives a hard copy of the magazine with her story in it. She calls Melissa and asks her why she shared Frances's story with Bobbi. He also explores the district of Clifton and visits a speakeasy-style bar

programme.title: Urban Secrets
programme.desc: Alan Cumming visits Liverpool, cutting through cliches about football and the Beatles to explore its less-celebrated treasures. Among his destinations are a 1930s banking building that has remained unaltered since closing in 2007, a well-appointed set of toilets and a recording studio hidden in a bar. He also discovers which former dictator once spent Christmas in the city

programme.title: Fish Town
programme.desc: Maritime Inn landlady Pat gets her first full house of the season as Brixham's tourist trade picks up for the summer - but refuses to hire anyone to help her with the extra work. Powerboat instructor Mike Weatherbee tries to enforce the laws of the sea, carpet-cleaner Dave Satchwill prepares to lead the town's popular ghost tour, and lone fisherman Dave Brown reveals his unusual methods

programme.title: Fish Town
programme.desc: August arrives and the fishermen of Brixham spend time ashore with their families as their boats are repaired - but summer proves costly for Jan, who loses £1,000 for every day his vessel is out of action. Apprentice deckhand Logan has to decide whether a life at sea is the right choice for him and his family, and seafood seller Jenny receives a surprise on her 65th birthday

Later, Danvers and Navarro confirm the location of Annie's murder, while the police chief levies a harsh punishment on Leah following her stepdaughter's act of protest

programme.title: Heated Rivalry
programme.desc: Kip and his friends watch on TV as Scott discusses his public coming out while accepting the MVP award. Shane drives to his cottage with Ilya, who shares the trauma of his mother's death by suicide

programme.title: Hacks
programme.desc: Ava tries to keep a secret from Deborah, but someone in Deborah's inner-circle leverages the truth. Meanwhile, Deborah stares down the barrel of her first radically honest show

programme.title: The Gilded Age
programme.desc: As Edison's illuminating electricity demonstration sparks conversation, George prepares for a legal battle and Bannister plots revenge

programme.title: The Gilded Age

programme.title: Homicide: Life on the Street
programme.desc: While investigating an assault, Ross and McCoy are faced with a dilemma when they realise they have no choice but to follow the lead of their only witness, a spurned admirer of the victim. Benjamin Bratt and Carey Lowell star in the second part of a special cross-over episode that began on Law and Order

programme.title: 28 Days Later (2002)
programme.desc: A man wakes from a coma to discover Britain has been struck by a terrifying experimental virus that sends victims into a homicidal rage. With a handful of other survivors, he heads for Manchester, the last outpost unaffected by the outbreak - but realises the virus is not the only threat. Danny Boyle's horror, starring Cillian Murphy, Naomie Harris, Brendan Gleeson and Christopher Eccleston

programme.title: The Man with Two Brains (1983)
programme.desc: A brilliant surgeon escapes the heartache and frustration of his failing marriage by falling in love with a bottled human brain - but needs to find a suitable body to implant it in. Sci-fi comedy, starring Steve Martin, Kathleen Turner and David Warner, with Sissy Spacek providing the voice of the doctor's beloved brain

programme.title: National Lampoon's Vacation (1983)
programme.desc: A bumbling all-American family man takes his wife and kids on a disaster-filled cross-country drive to California to visit famous theme park Walley World - but their chances of arriving at their destination in one piece are looking slim. Comedy, starring Chevy Chase, Beverly D'Angelo, Randy Quaid, Imogene Coca, Anthony Michael Hall, Christie Brinkley and John Candy

programme.title: The 40 Year-Old Virgin (2005)
programme.desc: A middle-aged shop worker reveals he has never been intimate with a woman, which prompts his shocked colleagues to set him up with a series of potentially passionate dates. When he falls for a single mother, his friends think he is right on course for a lusty liaison, but his new partner is not ready for a physical relationship. Judd Apatow's comedy, starring Steve Carell, Catherine Keener and Paul Rudd

programme.title: The 4:30 Movie (2024)
programme.desc: During the 1980s, New Jersey teenager Brian David and his friends sneak into different movie theatres. Writer/director Kevin Smith's semi-autobiographical teen comedy, starring Austin Zajur, Nicholas Cirillo and Reed Northrup

programme.title: Planes, Trains and Automobiles (1987)
programme.desc: A stressed businessman just wants to make it home to spend Thanksgiving with his family. However, when his flight is forced to land miles from its destination, he finds himself sharing a disaster-strewn journey across America with an amiable but slobbish salesman. Comedy road movie, starring Steve Martin, John Candy, Laila Robins, Kevin Bacon and Michael McKean

programme.title: Weird Science (1985)
programme.desc: Two sexually frustrated teenagers use their computer to design the perfect woman, but are ill prepared for the outcome when their creation comes to life. She decides to make men of them, by putting them in a series of situations that will force them to stand up for themselves. John Hughes' sci-fi comedy, starring Kelly LeBrock, Anthony Michael Hall, Ilan Mitchell-Smith, Robert Downey Jr and Bill Paxton

programme.title: Mars Attacks! (1996)
programme.desc: The inhabitants of Earth leap into action to repel an impending invasion by a seemingly invincible Martian army, which possesses superior weaponry, a thirst for destruction and a wicked sense of humour. Tim Burton's sci-fi comedy, starring Jack Nicholson, Glenn Close, Pierce Brosnan, Annette Bening, Natalie Portman, Danny DeVito and Tom Jones

programme.title: Beetlejuice Beetlejuice (2024)
programme.desc: A family tragedy woos Delia Deetz, daughter Lydia and rebellious teenage granddaughter Astrid back to the close-knit community of Winter River. Astrid accidentally opens a portal to the afterlife by summoning Beetlejuice three times. The mischievous demon ricochets between realms, wreaking havoc wherever he goes. Tim Burton's comedy sequel, starring Michael Keaton, Winona Ryder and Jenna Ortega

programme.title: This Is Spinal Tap (1984)
programme.desc: An ageing British rock band try to revive their fortunes by embarking on a US tour, with a film-maker on hand to record the mayhem. However, the big comeback soon turns into a series of disasters as tensions between the clueless rockers and their long-suffering manager reach breaking point. Spoof documentary, starring Michael McKean, Christopher Guest, Harry Shearer and Rob Reiner

programme.title: Spinal Tap II: The End Continues (2025)
programme.desc: Nigel Tufnel, David St Hubbins and Derek Smalls reunite after a 15-year break for one final concert. Their progress is charted once more by documentary maker Marti DiBergi, who offers perhaps too much perspective on their new direction. Comedy sequel, written by and starring Michael McKean, Harry Shearer, Christopher Guest and director Rob Reiner, in his final film. Kerry Godliman and Chris Addison also star with cameos by Paul McCartney, Elton John and Garth Brooks

programme.title: Yesterday (2019)
programme.desc: Jack, a struggling musician, gets hit by a bus during a mysterious event. When he regains consciousness, he finds that he is the only person who can remember the Beatles, and sets out to exploit the opportunity by passing their hits off as his own compositions. Danny Boyle's comedy, starring Himesh Patel and Lily James

programme.title: The Phoenician Scheme (2025)
programme.desc: Wealthy businessman Zsa-Zsa Korda appoints his only daughter, a nun, as sole heir to his estate. As Korda embarks on a new enterprise, they become the target of scheming tycoons, foreign terrorists and determined assassins. Writer/director Wes Anderson's comedy, starring Benicio del Toro, Mia Threapleton and Michael Cera

programme.title: Mickey 17 (2025)
programme.desc: In 2054, criminal Mickey Barnes signs up to be a worker who agrees to be reprinted every time he perishes. He flees Earth with friend and accomplice Timo on an interstellar mission to the remote colony of Niflheim, masterminded by Kenneth Marshall and his wife, Ylfa. The 17th iteration of Mickey is presumed dead after a close encounter with creatures on the planet's surface, but is saved from a grim fate, and when he arrives back at base, Mickey meets his reprinted self. Director Bong Joon Ho's sci-fi comedy, starring Robert Pattinson, Naomi Ackie, Mark Ruffalo, Toni Collette and Steven Yeun

programme.title: Sherlock Holmes (2009)
programme.desc: The eccentric detective and his confidant Dr Watson investigate the apparent resurrection of a criminal mastermind with links to the occult, who is carrying out a series of seemingly magical murders. The case is complicated by the reappearance of Holmes' duplicitous old flame Irene Adler. Action thriller, starring Robert Downey Jr, Jude Law, Rachel McAdams, Mark Strong and Eddie Marsan

programme.title: 28 Years Later (2025)
programme.desc: Decades since the rage virus took hold, the survivors live on a small island. When one of the group leaves on a mission into the mainland, he discovers secrets, wonders, and horrors that have mutated not only the infected but other survivors. Danny Boyle's horror thriller sequel, starring Jodie Comer, Aaron Taylor-Johnson, Ralph Fiennes and Rocco Haynes

programme.title: The 4:30 Movie (2024)
programme.desc: During the 1980s, New Jersey teenager Brian David and his friends sneak into different movie theatres. Writer/director Kevin Smith's semi-autobiographical teen comedy, starring Austin Zajur, Nicholas Cirillo and Reed Northrup

programme.title: The Phoenician Scheme (2025)
programme.desc: Wealthy businessman Zsa-Zsa Korda appoints his only daughter, a nun, as sole heir to his estate. As Korda embarks on a new enterprise, they become the target of scheming tycoons, foreign terrorists and determined assassins. Writer/director Wes Anderson's comedy, starring Benicio del Toro, Mia Threapleton and Michael Cera

programme.title: Yesterday (2019)
programme.desc: Jack, a struggling musician, gets hit by a bus during a mysterious event. When he regains consciousness, he finds that he is the only person who can remember the Beatles, and sets out to exploit the opportunity by passing their hits off as his own compositions. Danny Boyle's comedy, starring Himesh Patel and Lily James

programme.title: About Time (2013)
programme.desc: A single man gets the chance to turn his unhappy life around when his father reveals that they both have the ability to travel through time and change their personal history. He uses his newfound talent to try to improve his love life and win the heart of an American woman he has fallen for. Romantic fantasy comedy, with Domhnall Gleeson, Rachel McAdams and Bill Nighy

programme.title: Jerry Maguire (1996)
programme.desc: An idealistic sports agent produces a mission statement at work promising to treat clients fairly. Unfortunately, it costs him his job. Forced to set up an agency on his own, the only people prepared to stand by him are a lovelorn staff accountant and a footballer. Cameron Crowe's romantic comedy drama, with Tom Cruise, Renee Zellweger, Kelly Preston, Jonathan Lipnicki, and Cuba Gooding Jr, who won an Oscar for his performance

programme.title: Vanilla Sky (2001)
programme.desc: The laid-back lifestyle of an arrogant New York playboy is shattered when his unstable girlfriend drives off a bridge with him in the car. Although he survives, he struggles to put his life back together in the face of a series of strange events. Cameron Crowe's remake of Spanish thriller Open Your Eyes, starring Tom Cruise, Cameron Diaz, Kurt Russell, Jason Lee and Penelope Cruz, who also appeared in the original

programme.title: Mickey 17 (2025)
programme.desc: In 2054, criminal Mickey Barnes signs up to be a worker who agrees to be reprinted every time he perishes. He flees Earth with friend and accomplice Timo on an interstellar mission to the remote colony of Niflheim, masterminded by Kenneth Marshall and his wife, Ylfa. The 17th iteration of Mickey is presumed dead after a close encounter with creatures on the planet's surface, but is saved from a grim fate, and when he arrives back at base, Mickey meets his reprinted self. Director Bong Joon Ho's sci-fi comedy, starring Robert Pattinson, Naomi Ackie, Mark Ruffalo, Toni Collette and Steven Yeun

programme.title: 28 Years Later (2025)
programme.desc: Decades since the rage virus took hold, the survivors live on a small island. When one of the group leaves on a mission into the mainland, he discovers secrets, wonders, and horrors that have mutated not only the infected but other survivors. Danny Boyle's horror thriller sequel, starring Jodie Comer, Aaron Taylor-Johnson, Ralph Fiennes and Rocco Haynes

programme.title: Weapons (2025)
programme.desc: At 2.17am, 17 children from the class of Justine Gandy run into the night, arms outstretched, never to be seen again. The only child from the class who remains safely in their bed is Alex Lilly. Distraught parent Archer Graff joins other members of the local community in pointing a finger of blame at Ms Gandy for the shocking turn of events. While she pleads innocence, school principal Andrew Marcus seeks answers along with police officer Paul Morgan. Horror, starring Julia Garner, Josh Brolin and Alden Ehrenreich

programme.title: The Survivor
programme.desc: Drama based on the story of Harry Haft, a survivor of the Auschwitz concentration camp who boxed his fellow inmates to stay alive. Haunted by memories and guilt, he attempts to use high-profile fights against boxing legends like Rocky Marciano as a way to find his first love again. Directed by Barry Levinson and starring Ben Foster, John Leguizamo, Peter Sarsgaard and Danny DeVito

programme.title: Sherlock Holmes: A Game of Shadows (2011)
programme.desc: The detective investigates a series of seemingly unconnected crimes all around the world, and becomes convinced one man is behind them all - the elusive Professor Moriarty. He sets forth on an international journey to bring the evil mastermind to justice, but finds he is dealing with a foe who is his intellectual equal. Action thriller sequel, starring Robert Downey Jr, Jude Law, Noomi Rapace and Jared Harris

programme.title: Him
programme.desc: Cameron Cade is predicted to be the next great quarterback of American football. During a night-time training session, he suffers a traumatic brain injury. Cameron agrees to attend a week-long boot camp run by retiring star quarterback Isaiah White at his secluded desert compound. However, Cade's life soon turns into a nightmare. Supernatural horror, starring Marlon Wayans and Tyriq Withers

programme.title: The Bride! (2026)
programme.desc: In 1930s Chicago, maverick scientist Dr Euphronius gives life to a murdered woman, sparking romance, police interest and radical change. Writer/director Maggie Gyllenhaal's horror based on The Bride of Frankenstein, starring Jessie Buckley, Christian Bale and Annette Bening

programme.title: Honey Don't (2025)
programme.desc: California private detective Honey O'Donahue delves into a series of unusual deaths connected to a mysterious church. Co-writer/director Ethan Coen's comedy mystery, starring Margaret Qualley, Aubrey Plaza and Chris Evans

programme.title: Tafiti - Across The Desert (2025)
programme.desc: A young meerkat's grandpa is bitten by a snake, so he sets off to find a magical flower for the cure. Animated adventure, featuring the voices of Cosima Henman, Steve Hudson and Kathleen Renish

programme.title: The Shadow's Edge (2025)
programme.desc: A brilliant cyber-criminal gang evades the police's state-of-the-art surveillance system with a haul worth billions. Action thriller, starring Jackie Chan, Zhang Zifeng and Tony Leung Ka-fai
