programme.title: Murders That Shocked the Nation
programme.desc: The presenter explores the case of John Straffen, who killed two young girls in the summer of 1951. He was found to be unfit to plead and committed to Broadmoor Hospital, but during a brief escape from the facility in 1952 he killed again. It was determined that the cause of his behaviour was severe brain damage, traced back to an attack of encephalitis as a child in India. When he died in 2007, he was the longest-serving prisoner in Britain

programme.title: MoviePass, MovieCrash
programme.desc: The story of MoviePass, the company that offered cinema tickets at a discounted rate. It was a movie lover's dreaam, but in just eight years it went from being the fastest-growing subscription service since Spotify to total bankruptcy. This documentary chronicles the visionary mission of its co-founders, its early success and how it finally failed due to mismanagement and corporate greed

Featuring re-creations of his earlier stunts at Paris's Notre Dame cathedral and Sydney Harbour Bridge

programme.title: Big Cats: An Amazing Animal Family
programme.desc: Patrick Aryee concludes his two-part exploration of the feline family tree, revealing how the Canadian lynx learned to thrive in the snow and spread out, evolving and inhabiting new parts of the Americas. In Belize, he mounts an experiment that showcases the margay's supersharp senses and gravity-defying acrobatics, and heads to the training track with a cheetah to find out what speed it is capable of. Finally, Patrick tells the story of the cat's domestication in Essaouira, Morocco, believed to be one of the first places where one particular species first gave up life in the wild in return for food, warmth, security and a home

programme.desc: Glenn and Tara are determined to reach Terminus, but Abraham is reluctant. Daryl struggles as he tries to adjust to the Claimers' rules. Post-apocalyptic drama following the exploits of a former County Sheriff struggling to survive in the aftermath of a zombie epidemic. Starring Andrew Lincoln

programme.title: Britannia
programme.desc: In the final episode, druid brothers Veran and Harka finally face each other in a dramatic challenge. Meanwhile, Cait also talks with Quane

programme.title: Stargate Origins: Catherine (2018)
programme.desc: Catherine Langford enlists the help of two soldiers to venture into the Stargate and save the world from an unimaginable darkness. Sci-fi adventure prequel, starring Ellie Gall

programme.title: The Ark
programme.desc: The crew arrives somewhere that seems too good to be true... and it just might be

programme.title: The Walking Dead
programme.desc: Glenn and Tara are determined to reach Terminus, but Abraham is reluctant. Daryl struggles as he tries to adjust to the Claimers' rules. Post-apocalyptic drama following the exploits of a former County Sheriff struggling to survive in the aftermath of a zombie epidemic. Starring Andrew Lincoln

programme.title: Britannia
programme.desc: In the final episode, druid brothers Veran and Harka finally face each other in a dramatic challenge. Meanwhile, Cait also talks with Quane

programme.title: Most Haunted
programme.desc: Yvette Fielding and the team visit Aberglasney House - a derelict property in Carmarthenshire, south Wales

programme.title: Bonus Track (2023)
programme.desc: An aspiring teenage musician teams up with the son of a rock star to try and win the school talent show. Comedy, starring Joe Anders, Samuel Small and Susan Wokoma

programme.title: Mascarpone (2021)
programme.desc: A young gay man who must carve out a new path for himself after his husband - who he depends on financially - dumps him. Italian comedy drama, starring Giancarlo Commare and Eduardo Valdarnini

programme.title: The Man with the Answers (2021)
programme.desc: A former diving champion and an eccentric student take an adventurous road trip of rediscovery. Romantic drama, starring Vasilis Magouliotis, Anton Weil and Stella Fyrogeni. In Greek, Italian, German and English

programme.title: Beach Rats (2017)
programme.desc: Powerful drama about a troubled Brooklyn teen aimlessly drifting through summer while struggling to come to terms with his own desires. Starring Harris Dickinson

programme.title: You Can Live Forever (2022)
programme.desc: After moving into a Jehovah's Witness community, lesbian teenager Jamie begins a life-changing love affair with the daughter of a prominent Witness Elder. Drama, starring Anwen O'Driscoll and June Laporte

programme.title: The United States Vs. Billie Holiday (2021)
programme.desc: The acclaimed singer is targeted by the Federal Department of Narcotics with an undercover sting operation led by Federal Agent Jimmy Fletcher, with who she had a tumultuous affair. Director Lee Daniels' biopic based on the novel by Johann Hari, starring Andra Day, Trevante Rhodes and Natasha Lyonne

programme.title: Downton Abbey: The Grand Finale (2025)
programme.desc: In the summer of 1930, the Crawleys grapple with the threat of social disgrace when Mary finds herself at the centre of a public scandal. Romantic drama, starring Joely Richardson, Joanne Froggatt and Paul Giamatti

programme.title: Monsoon (2019)
programme.desc: A British-Vietnamese man returns to his birth country for the first time in more than 30 years. He was only eight when he and his family escaped Saigon as boat refugees during the Vietnam-American war. Now he embarks on a personal journey from Saigon to Hanoi to find a place to scatter his parents' ashes. Drama, starring Henry Golding, Parker Sawyers, David Tran and Molly Harris

programme.title: Wicked (2024)
programme.desc: Green-skinned Elphaba Thropp becomes part of the new intake at Shiz University, and shares a room with fellow spell-caster Galinda Upland. It's the beginning of a friendship which culminates in an epic showdown as Elphaba transforms a renegade witch with a skill for bursting into show-stopping songs, like Defying Gravity. Part one of The Wizard of Oz prequel based on the hit stage musical, starring Cynthia Erivo, Ariana Grande-Butera, Jonathan Bailey, Michelle Yeoh and Jeff Goldblum

programme.title: Blue Moon (2025)
programme.desc: Set over the course of one turbulent night in March 1943, Lorenz Hart, the waspish wordsmith responsible for The Lady Is a Tramp and My Funny Valentine, finds his life unravelling on the opening evening of his ex-partner's show, Oklahoma! Director Richard Linklater's biographical comedy drama, starring Ethan Hawke, Margaret Qualley, Bobby Cannavale and Andrew Scott

programme.title: On Swift Horses (2025)
programme.desc: Mild-mannered war veteran Lee Walker plans a life of quiet domesticity in California with his wife Muriel and younger brother Julius. Restless after an early discharge from the US Army, Julius steals money from Lee to seek his fortune in Nevada, where he lands a job in security at a casino and sparks forbidden desire with co-worker Henry, which is consummated away from prying eyes. Back in their hotel room, Julius and Henry fantasise about using the same underhand tactics as card sharps to line their pockets. Drama, starring Daisy Edgar-Jones, Jacob Elordi and Will Poulter

programme.title: Rocketman (2019)
programme.desc: Elton John enters an addiction rehabilitation session, and recounts his life in a flashback, covering his days as young Reginald Dwight growing up in 1950s Britain. He takes piano lessons, makes his way into the Royal Academy of Music, and hones his craft in local pubs before becoming one of the world's biggest pop stars. Golden Globe-winning biopic, starring Taron Egerton, Jamie Bell and Richard Madden

programme.title: Carmen & Lola (2018)
programme.desc: After two Roma women's lives collide in the suburbs of Madrid, they are forced to make a difficult choice between community and love. Award-winning LGBTQ+ drama, starring Rosy Rodríguez and Zaira Romero

programme.title: Beach Rats (2017)
programme.desc: Powerful drama about a troubled Brooklyn teen aimlessly drifting through summer while struggling to come to terms with his own desires. Starring Harris Dickinson

Director Richard Linklater's biographical comedy drama, starring Ethan Hawke, Margaret Qualley, Bobby Cannavale and Andrew Scott

programme.title: Kindred (2020)
programme.desc: When her boyfriend dies suddenly in an accident, psychologically fragile mother-to-be Charlotte is taken in by his family - but they seem increasingly obsessed with her every move. Her suspicions grow and panic builds - but just how far will they go in their desire to control her and her unborn baby? Horror, starring Tamara Lawrance, Jack Lowden and Fiona Shaw

programme.title: I Don't Understand You (2025)
programme.desc: Stranded in rural Italy, an American couple on the verge of adopting tries to reconnect during a disastrous vacation. Comedic horror, starring Nick Kroll, Andrew Rannells and Amanda Seyfried

programme.title: Rocketman (2019)
programme.desc: Elton John enters an addiction rehabilitation session, and recounts his life in a flashback, covering his days as young Reginald Dwight growing up in 1950s Britain. He takes piano lessons, makes his way into the Royal Academy of Music, and hones his craft in local pubs before becoming one of the world's biggest pop stars. Golden Globe-winning biopic, starring Taron Egerton, Jamie Bell and Richard Madden

programme.title: On Swift Horses (2025)

Back in their hotel room, Julius and Henry fantasise about using the same underhand tactics as card sharps to line their pockets. Drama, starring Daisy Edgar-Jones, Jacob Elordi and Will Poulter

programme.title: Interview with the Vampire (1994)
programme.desc: A man who claims to be 200 years old tells a reporter his story of how he was bitten during an encounter with a decadent and charismatic vampire in 18th-century New Orleans. He relates how his respect for the sanctity of human life clashed with his blood-lust, and how the pair came to be adoptive parents to a young girl. Gothic horror based on the novel by Anne Rice, with Tom Cruise, Brad Pitt, Kirsten Dunst, Antonio Banderas and Christian Slater

programme.title: Cuckoo (2024)
programme.desc: A 17-year-old moves with her family to a resort where things are not what they seem. Writer/director Tilman Slinger's horror thriller, starring Hunter Schafer, Jan Bluthardt and Dan Stevens

programme.title: Dog Day Afternoon (1975)
programme.desc: Hapless Sonny and his sidekick Sal have a plan to rob a Brooklyn bank. The novice crooks thought they would be in and out in 10 minutes, but instead find themselves stuck in an ongoing hostage situation, with the police and the media laying siege to the bank. Sidney Lumet's fact-based drama, starring Al Pacino, John Cazale and Charles Durning.

programme.title: The Lost Boys (1987)
programme.desc: Two teenage brothers move with their mother to a coastal town in California, where the eldest falls in with a local biker gang. He soon discovers that his new friends are vampires and he is turning into one himself, forcing his younger brother to team up with a pair of heavily armed comic shop employees to battle the undead and save his soul. Comedy horror, starring Kiefer Sutherland, Jason Patric, Dianne Wiest, Corey Feldman and Corey Haim

programme.title: Pillion (2025)
programme.desc: A directionless man is swept off his feet when an enigmatic biker takes him on as his submissive. Director Harry Lighton's Bafta-nominated comedy, starring Alexander Skarsgard, Harry Melling and Douglas Hodge

programme.title: Judge Judy
programme.desc: A motorist admits he ran a stop sign and accidentally killed a small dog; pet owners sues for the cost of a new dog and cremation expenses.

programme.title: Judge Judy
programme.desc: A $13,000 family trip to Disneyworld ends with ex-lovers fighting over who owes what; a man insists he never agreed to shell out so much money.

programme.title: Judge Judy
programme.desc: An incident in juvenile hall leaves a teen with a 25K settlement; he claims his father sold his newly purchased vehicles when he was jailed again.

programme.title: Judge Judy
programme.desc: Punchy reality show about a female judge that has made a real impact on the American justice system. Judge Judy is real, honest and keen to get to the bottom of emotional cases.

programme.title: Judge Judy
programme.desc: A man claims the mother of his children emptied his bank account and sold his tools used to make cars for wounded warriors; she claims he abandoned her.

programme.title: Judge Judy
programme.desc: A boat owner claims the Captain of the SS Enterprise damaged his smaller vessel after a fishing 'turf war? on the high seas.

programme.title: Judge Judy
programme.desc: A 12-year-old, with a history of discipline issues, claims she was pinched and insulted by an adult visiting her classroom.

programme.title: Judge Judy
programme.desc: A woman takes issue with her boyfriend renting a car for a female friend; he defends himself by saying he's a sucker for those in need.

programme.title: Britain's Almost Perfect Murders
programme.desc: Nurse Beverley Allitt murdered four children and harmed nine more in Lincolnshire, abusing trust until a pattern of deaths exposed her.

programme.title: UK Crime Files: Blackpool Double Murder
programme.desc: Geoffrey Wansell explores two eerily similar murders that take place ten years apart in Blackpool when two elderly women are killed.

programme.title: UK Crime Files: A Random Murder
programme.desc: A look into the case of Kevin Bennett, who was murdered in 2014 by Connor and Brandon Doran and friend Simon Evans, after they attacked him as he slept rough.

programme.title: Britain's Countryside Killers
programme.desc: The story of Lindsay Birbeck, who, in August 2019, was murdered by 16-year-old Rocky Marciano Price after he targeted her during a walk.

programme.title: Donal MacIntyre's UK Killer Evidence
programme.desc: A body is found in a pub freezer in the UK and police uncover suspicious texts. The main suspect is on the run, with forensic evidence pointing to his guilt.

programme.title: UK Crime Files: Lady Killer
programme.desc: A look at how the murder of an actress triggered a nationwide manhunt. But whilst Neville Heath was on the run in Bournemouth, another body was found.

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The sleuthing priest investigates the mysterious death of a beekeeper. Nigel Planer guest stars in the 1950s-set murder mystery series

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Humphrey and Martha's romantic weekend on a remote island is cut short when the hotel owner is stabbed to death in his room in what appears to be an attempted robbery. But since the flower beds outside the window are undisturbed, Humphrey is certain that one of the victim's associates played a part in his death. There's just one problem - since the DI had a direct view of the only staircase leading up to the room, he is the alibi for all the suspects. Meanwhile, JP worries about his marriage and seeks relationship advice from Dwayne

programme.title: Hudson & Rex
programme.desc: When two hikers are discovered murdered near a remote coastal trail, the team is on the hunt for a fugitive duo wanted in a series of grisly campsite murders

programme.desc: Brackenreid and some fellow inspectors unwind in a remote lodge, but their respite turns deadly when they find themselves hunted by a killer

programme.title: The Mentalist
programme.desc: The team investigates when a struggling gold prospector is killed, but Lisbon is injured during the opening stages of the case and Hightower takes her place. Simon Baker, Robin Tunney and Aunjanue Ellis star

programme.desc: The team uncovers distorted loyalties that form when personal ties meet the trappings of fame when a troubled young pop star is suspected of killing her fiance

programme.desc: Morgan has become obsessed with ruining Carrie, but when he is found dead, the detective finds herself under suspicion of murder. With a motive, incriminating fingerprints, and CCTV footage, her career is jeopardised, forcing her to push her remarkable memory to the limit to prove she has been set up

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The president of the local cricket club is found shot dead in the middle of the pitch after a recent victory. programme.title: The Mentalist
programme.desc: Agent LaRoche suspects Hightower of the murder of a police killer, who was burned to death in custody, after she reveals that she was sleeping with one of his victims. programme.desc: Carrie's latest case brings back painful memories of her sister's murder. Noticing a familiar face, she desperately chases him for answers and ends up putting her relationship with Al in jeopardy, and her life at risk

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Part one of two. A man is found murdered on his boat five miles out at sea, with no possible way a killer could have escaped. Kris Marshall stars

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The second of a two-part story picks up where things left off last week, with Humphrey, Florence and Dwayne having swapped the sun and sand of the Caribbean for the cold and noise of London. Their latest investigation has taken them back to the detective inspector's home town - but they've hit a brick wall with the discovery of another dead body. And now they have two murders to solve. Meanwhile, Dwayne has an emotional encounter and Humphrey receives advice from an unexpected source that helps him reach a big decision. Crime drama, starring Kris Marshall, Danny John-Jules and Josephine Jobert, with guest appearances from Ardal O'Hanlon and Sally Bretton

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Actress and writer Daisy May Cooper, quiz guru Richard Osman, actress, comedian and writer Diane Morgan, and journalist and broadcaster Rory Reid join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As ever, the participants aim to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Guests Matt Lucas, Jerry Springer and Cariad Lloyd join regular panellist Alan Davies and host Sandi Toksvig to contemplate the theme 'noodles' in this extended episode. As usual, correctness and even intelligence go out of the window in this quiz, in which the questions are so difficult points are awarded for the answers its questionmaster finds most interesting

programme.desc: A new recruit fresh out of training school makes her mark when she uncovers 12kg of cocaine hidden in a car that she has stopped on one of the bridges

programme.title: Border Force: America's Gatekeepers
programme.desc: Customs and Border Protection officers think they have spotted a trend when they seize drugs from behind the dashboards of two cars. A Mexican woman asks officers for protection

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef heads to Berlin, where history and the avant garde dwell side by side. A city that once made history with its divisive wall and Checkpoint Charlie now teems with an overwhelming array of innovative chefs offering delicious seasonal creations, such as pureed sunflower seeds, Jerusalem artichoke flans and crushed frozen pine nuts. Loved by the likes of Greta Garbo, Charlie Chaplin and Auguste Escoffier there's still a cornucopia of choice for those with a more traditional temperament with eisbein, meatballs and sausages topping every menu in the German capital

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef heads to Cadiz in Spain, where he samples two of the city's most revered culinary stars - fresh tuna and Manzanilla sherry. Rick is captivated by the narrow winding streets as he walks in the footsteps of the ancient Phoenician and Arab traders, whose influence on the city can still be seen to this day. Back home, he prepares flamenco eggs and arroz verde, or green rice, made with garlic, parsley, clams and prawns, producing the vibrant colours of the Spanish flag

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson tests Toyota pick-up the Hilux with a series of extraordinary tasks, and puts the pedal to the metal with Mazda's sports coupe, the Wankel rotary-engined RX8. Meanwhile, James May puts the Fiat Panda through its paces, and Richard Hammond checks out classic cars in waiting - the Mercedes 190E and the VW Corrado. Simon Cowell is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson reviews Citroen's C2, while Richard Hammond compares convertible models of the Renault Megane and Peugeot 307, and James May looks back at Aston Martin's V8 Vantage. The team also tries to destroy a Toyota Hilux, and Sanjeev Bhaskar is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Cameras follow old friends Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer as they continue on their angling trip round the UK. In the Monsal Valley in Derbyshire, Paul tries to teach Bob how to fly fish for wild rainbow trout, and the pair reflect on their humble beginnings, their careers and their comedy contemporaries. Later, Bob cooks Paul his very personal recipe of tuna melanie on the river bank, and it soon transpires his cooking is a lot more successful than his fishing

programme.title: The Lakes with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The adventurer explores the wild and rugged coast of Cumbria and learns how central the county is to many of the challenges facing Britain. He visits Sellafield, which is home to hundreds of ageing and disused nuclear facilities and meets people tasked with clearing and securing more than half a century of Britain's nuclear waste

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers revisit the 1970s, a decade which is all too often unfairly stigmatised for its food. They show how a well-prepared chicken cordon bleu and banoffee pie can be a contemporary gastronomic delight, before heading to a restaurant whose menu harks back to that era

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef heads to Berlin, where history and the avant garde dwell side by side. programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing

Later, Bob cooks Paul his very personal recipe of tuna melanie on the river bank, and it soon transpires his cooking is a lot more successful than his fishing

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedians Jimmy Carr and Marcus Brigstocke, TV presenter Terry Christian and singer Jamelia join team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show, in which they try to dupe their opponents with plausible lies about themselves. Presented by Rob Brydon

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Rob Brydon hosts a festive edition of the comedy panel show in which the participants aim to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory. Joining team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack for the Christmas fun are comedian James Acaster, singer-songwriter Lily Allen, Peter Kay's Car Share star Sian Gibson and former Slade frontman Noddy Holder. They all reveal amazing stories, but are they telling the truth, or making it all up?

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Guests Matt Lucas, Jerry Springer and Cariad Lloyd join regular panellist Alan Davies and host Sandi Toksvig to contemplate the theme 'noodles' in this extended episode. As usual, correctness and even intelligence go out of the window in this quiz, in which the questions are so difficult points are awarded for the answers its questionmaster finds most interesting

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Lorraine Kelly hosts an extended episode of the current affairs quiz, with team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton joined by comedian Maisie Adam and journalist Matt Chorley

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats
programme.desc: Irreverent panel show hosted by Jimmy Carr, in which guests Krishnan Guru-Murthy, Phil Wang, Gabby Logan and Rosie Jones answer questions based on opinion polls

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain hosts as regular panellist Hugh Dennis looks back on the latest events in news and politics, with the help of Tom Allen, Angela Barnes, Ed Byrne, Milton Jones and Thanyia Moore. Yet again, stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on one another

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?

programme.title: Dave Gorman: Modern Life Is Goodish
programme.desc: The comedian consults his beloved laptop computer as he examines celebrity culture and star lookalikes

programme.title: Parks and Recreation
programme.desc: Ben is tasked with helping Tom with his new business. Meanwhile, Leslie tries to keep Ron away from the controlling powers of his ex-wife Tammy. Comedy, starring Amy Poehler and Nick Offerman

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Border Force: America's Gatekeepers
programme.desc: Officers in Hidalgo are forced to close the bridge when it's stormed by dozens of asylum seekers trying to jump the queue of people waiting to seek refuge in the USA

programme.title: Border Force: America's Gatekeepers
programme.desc: A 12-year-old boy turns up at one of the bridges on his own, claiming that his mother has been kidnapped by the cartels and he has been living on the streets

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef visits Vienna, the city that once ran the Austro-Hungarian Empire and continues to be home to comfort dishes such as tafelspitz and goulash, and which gave its name to one of Europe's most popular dishes - the Wiener schnitzel. Rick learns the essential etiquette to the Austrian capital's coffee house culture and indulges in its sweet tooth by enjoying a plate or two of apple strudel and Sacher torte. He also finds time to enjoy the traditional music of the country, typically Mozart and Strauss

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef heads to the north-eastern Greek city of Thessaloniki, which nestles on the northern shores of the Aegean Sea. The city is revered as the gourmet capital of Greece and birthplace to possibly the greatest warrior general that ever lived - Alexander. Rick explores a city of many identities, boasting a young student population apparently hungry for dishes that reflect the freshest of seafood. Back home, he prepares veal and aubergine stew after meeting the doyenne of Greek cooking, Vafa Alexiadou

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The presenters chooses their favourite British car and examine Germany's contribution to the motor industry. Jeremy Clarkson test-drives Porsche's off-roader, the Cayenne, while Richard Hammond burns up the road in the SLR, one of Mercedes' fastest road vehicles, and James May examines some of the worst cars on the market. Rory Bremner is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson compares the 2005 Mercedes SL with the same model from 1972 and the Audi TT with Ford's classic Capri V6, while Richard Hammond looks at the Nissan Micra. The Star in a Reasonably Priced Car is Johnny Vegas, and the team travels to Germany in a bid to break the saloon car speed record

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Cameras follow old friends Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer as they continue on their angling trip round the UK. This time, Paul and Bob fish the river Wensum in Norfolk for roach, but as the pair struggle up and down the riverbanks, they begin to realise how age is starting to take its toll on their physicality. After staying overnight in a disused train station and visiting a local pub, the pair decide to challenge each other to a fishing competition the following day, where the winner gets a very special culinary treat

programme.title: Mediterranean with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: A journey around the region, examining the challenges currently facing the nations in the area. Simon begins in Malta, uncovering the country's reputation for organised crime, before moving on to the southern coast of Italy, where he joins police officers exploring underground tunnels used by crime bosses on the run. Finally, he arrives in Albania and discovers an ancient tradition of revenge where children can pay the price for their parents' actions

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers celebrate British dairy products, preparing a classic cheese and onion turnover, a trinity cream and a number of traditional milk puddings. They also look at the history of school milk and Cheddar cheese

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef visits Vienna, the city that once ran the Austro-Hungarian Empire and continues to be home to comfort dishes such as tafelspitz and goulash, and which gave its name to one of Europe's most popular dishes - the Wiener schnitzel. Simon begins in Malta, uncovering the country's reputation for organised crime, before moving on to the southern coast of Italy, where he joins police officers exploring underground tunnels used by crime bosses on the run. Finally, he arrives in Albania and discovers an ancient tradition of revenge where children can pay the price for their parents' actions

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers celebrate British dairy products, preparing a classic cheese and onion turnover, a trinity cream and a number of traditional milk puddings. They also look at the history of school milk and Cheddar cheese

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef visits Vienna, the city that once ran the Austro-Hungarian Empire and continues to be home to comfort dishes such as tafelspitz and goulash, and which gave its name to one of Europe's most popular dishes - the Wiener schnitzel. Rick learns the essential etiquette to the Austrian capital's coffee house culture and indulges in its sweet tooth by enjoying a plate or two of apple strudel and Sacher torte. He also finds time to enjoy the traditional music of the country, typically Mozart and Strauss

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef heads to the north-eastern Greek city of Thessaloniki, which nestles on the northern shores of the Aegean Sea. The city is revered as the gourmet capital of Greece and birthplace to possibly the greatest warrior general that ever lived - Alexander. Rick explores a city of many identities, boasting a young student population apparently hungry for dishes that reflect the freshest of seafood. Back home, he prepares veal and aubergine stew after meeting the doyenne of Greek cooking, Vafa Alexiadou

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Cameras follow old friends Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer as they continue on their angling trip round the UK. This time, Paul and Bob fish the river Wensum in Norfolk for roach, but as the pair struggle up and down the riverbanks, they begin to realise how age is starting to take its toll on their physicality. After staying overnight in a disused train station and visiting a local pub, the pair decide to challenge each other to a fishing competition the following day, where the winner gets a very special culinary treat

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: TV presenter Claudia Winkleman, broadcaster Clive Anderson, comedian Jason Manford and comedienne Miranda Hart join team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show, in which they try to dupe their opponents with plausible lies about themselves. Presented by Rob Brydon

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedians Rhod Gilbert and Ellie Taylor, musician Ronan Keating and media personality Georgia Toffolo join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As ever, the participants aim to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Guests Johnny Vegas, Ronni Ancona and Jimmy Carr join regular panellist Alan Davies and host Sandi Toksvig to contemplate the theme 'naval navigation' in this extended episode. As usual, correctness and even intelligence go out of the window in this quiz, in which the questions are so difficult points are awarded for the answers its questionmaster finds most interesting

programme.title: The Way Out
programme.desc: It's a fiery finale as the teams race to rescue the hapless Donna from a burning flat. With a blaze to put out and puzzles to ponder, they must keep calm as the heat rises

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats
programme.desc: Irreverent panel show hosted by Jimmy Carr, with guests Pamela Anderson, Kerry Godliman, Ella Eyre and Joe Wilkinson joining team captains Rob Beckett and Aisling Bea

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain hosts as regular panellist Hugh Dennis looks back on the latest events in news and politics, with the help of Tom Allen, Maff Brown, Ria Lina, Ed Byrne and Rhys James. Yet again, stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on one another

However, the deceased's daughter, a nurse, claims that her mother was dead before he even arrived. Meanwhile, Lily is called to a house where the body of a man has been found on top of a bonfire in the back garden

programme.desc: Carlos arrives at the hospital with a patient and attempts to make peace with Tricia for abandoning her - but neither she nor Mark is ready to forgive and forget. Donna gets a new job, and Mubbs finds his wild lifestyle catching up to him when he suffers a heart attack. Connie announces her latest grand scheme at a team meeting, but the plan does not go down well with the staff

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Part one of two. Ruth's relationship with Jay seems stronger than ever, but a comment made by a stranger causes her old insecurities to resurface. Linda's former adversary Damon visits the hospital and reveals he knows she lied about baby Evie's parentage, while fire officer Frank identifies potential hazards around the ED, shortly before the fire alarm begins to sound throughout the building. Guest starring Sean Blowers (London's Burning), with Georgia Taylor and Christine Tremarco

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Lennox's loyalties are tested when he sees his wife at the home of a suspect

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: The Mitchells struggle to hold their family together, and the Slaters face a worrying visit from Social Services after the tragedy at the Vic. How will Pat cope without Peggy?

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: The results of the paternity test are finally revealed. Charlie's worry for his family leads to a heartbreaking confrontation, and Shirley makes a brave decision

programme.title: Sister Boniface Mysteries
programme.desc: Grace Pearson is found dead at a local festival. Detective Inspector Sam Gillespie - to the bewilderment of newly arrived Detective Sergeant Felix Livingstone - uses Sister Boniface as his scientific adviser. She determines that Pearson died by suffocation after her head was pushed into sawdust. The investigation leads to the three Bellamy siblings, at whose home Pearson was a housemaid. Crime drama, starring Lorna Watson and Max Brown, with Belinda Lang and Robert Daws

programme.title: The Doctor Blake Mysteries
programme.desc: The medic investigates when a businessman is found drowned in the swimming baths after the grand unveiling of his plans for a local theme park about the Ballarat gold rush

programme.desc: The dodgy dealer is asked to furnish a restaurant, but the items he acquires turn out to be stolen. To make matters worse, the owners turn up for the establishment's opening night. John Wells guest stars, with Ian McShane

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: Tom makes a shocking proposition to the ailing Diana, while Jane seeks advice on becoming a nun and Geoffrey poses as a Hell's Angel in a misguided effort to impress his estranged wife Marion. Comedy, starring Graham Crowden, Stephanie Cole and Janine Duvitski

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Young-at-heart trio Compo, Clegg and Foggy gamely volunteer to investigate reports of something sinister stirring in the woods. Rural comedy, starring Peter Sallis, Bill Owen and Brian Wilde

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Compo hires a motorbike from Auntie Wainwright and takes Nora Batty for a ride in it. The disaster-fraught trip sees Nora chasing after him for once - but only because she is hurtling toward him in an out-of-control sidecar. Rural comedy, starring Bill Owen and Kathy Staff

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: A friend's lack of interest in life prompts Compo, never one to shy away from danger, to volunteer to test Wesley's heavy-duty sucker and blower, which can only mean disaster. Roy Clarke's comedy, with Bill Owen, Gordon Wharmby and Frank Thornton

programme.desc: It's opening night and the world's most extravagant art lovers gather, but as the gala begins, the night takes an unexpected turn and secrets are revealed

programme.desc: Part one of two. When a popular security guard is stabbed to death, leaving behind a terminally ill wife, it appears as though he died as a result of a drug deal gone wrong

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The detectives investigate the murder of an East German immigrant, but all they have to go on are his mysterious final words, which translate as 'blue flower'. As they piece together his remarkable story, Sandra tries to gain the trust of his estranged daughter. Crime drama, starring Amanda Redman

programme.title: Dalziel & Pascoe
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. A housewife dies of chemical poisoning during her weekly shopping trip and the detectives immediately suspect they have a case of terrorism on their hands, However, a second death points them toward a health retreat with a troubled past and a mysterious woman named Barbara Lennox. Stephen Tompkinson guest stars

programme.title: Doctors

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: George tries to get to know Lily better but puts her foot in it by telling her about Daniel's gambling and womanising past, and Heston takes Andy for an activity day in the woods. programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Tricia prepares for her operation, only to discover it has been cancelled. Meanwhile, Lola orders Mubbs to clean up his lifestyle before he suffers another heart attack, but the stubborn doctor won't listen and persuades Matt and Dean to smuggle bacon sandwiches into the hospital. However, he soon changes his tune after collapsing on the stairs

programme.title: Casualty

As Jordan issues an evacuation order, Dylan and Zoe are trapped in resus and Jay confronts Ruth in her office, oblivious to the chaos below. Starring Georgia Taylor and Ben Turner

programme.desc: First episode of the Second World War drama set in a Japanese PoW camp holding only female inmates, with the pampered British inhabitants of Singapore blissfully unaware of the looming onslaught of the Japanese. Stephanie Beacham and Ann Bell star

programme.desc: When a lottery winner is kidnapped and held to ransom, Blake uses his keen instincts and personal touch to sift through the suspects

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Truly decides to put his extensive police training to good use by roping Compo and Clegg into his newly formed active rescue team, and finds there is only one thing stopping them becoming heroes - the lack of a casualty. Long-running comedy, starring Frank Thornton

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.sub-title: Oh Howard, We Should Get One of Those

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: A mysterious saboteur tries to stop Kembleford winning the Three Counties choir competition. Return of the 1950s-set crime drama based on the stories by GK Chesterton, starring Mark Williams as the Roman Catholic priest who attempts to solve various misdemeanours

programme.title: Art Detectives
programme.desc: Mick and Shazia investigate the discovery of a Viking hoard after a museum curator is suspicious about the circumstances and summons Mick for a second opinion

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: In a special edition of the detective drama that features only two members of the team, Gerry Standing and Steve McAndrew travel to Glasgow, where a new Ucos section is being set up. While there, they agree to assist the investigation into the 1993 unsolved murder of James Soutar, the wealthy owner of a string of betting shops. Cathy Sinclair, one of the beneficiaries of the bookie's will at his death, had a care-home upbringing in common with Soutar, but she claims to have no idea who he was or why she was left £15,000. McAndrew has a personal stake in the case - the original investigating officer was corrupt cop Frank McNair, who had an affair with his wife, and their paths soon cross. The London-based officers' questions begin to unsettle someone and a campaign of victimisation against them begins, but it's a revelation from McAndrew's girlfriend Charley that reawakens a long-dormant scandal and clears the path to a solution. Dennis Waterman and Denis Lawson star

programme.title: Dalziel & Pascoe
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The detectives are called out to a robbery where a security guard is found dead. As they try to determine whether his demise was an accident or murder, they expose a complex web of deception among his friends - or enemies. Meanwhile, Lateef uncovers evidence of an international exchange racket. Art Malik guest stars

programme.title: Harry Wild

While recovering at the home of her police detective son Charlie, she looks at his files and realises his latest investigation bears similarities to an obscure Elizabethan play. But when her findings are rebuffed, she decides to find the killer-with aid from a surprising source. Drama, starring Jane Seymour

programme.title: Trawlermen
programme.desc: The skipper of one of the smallest boats in the Peterhead fleet must decide whether to head back to harbour or brave the treacherous sea following a severe weather warning. programme.title: David Jason's Great British Inventions
programme.desc: The actor examines British innovations in the history of flight, from the first glider to the modern jet suit, and discovers how Britain contributed to the moon landing

programme.title: David Jason's Great British Inventions
programme.desc: The actor examines British innovations in the home from the lightbulb to the lawnmower, and discovers the role Queen Victoria played in creating the telephone

programme.title: Warbird Workshop
programme.desc: A Sea King rescue helicopter requires a new engine installing, as well as giant rotor blades and a 1970s computer system reviving, before it can return to the skies

programme.title: Warbird Workshop
programme.desc: On a wartime bomber base in Lincolnshire, veteran engineer Gerry Cooper rebuilds two pre-war biplanes with glorious histories; the US Navy N3N and the Morane Saulnier 315

programme.title: David Jason's Great British Inventions
programme.desc: The actor examines British innovations in the history of engineering. In the first episode, David takes a look at wheeled vehicles from the Penny Farthing bicycle to Stephenson's rocket - as well as being reunited with an original Reliant Regal Supervan, as used in Only Fools and Horses

programme.title: Robson Green's Weekend Escapes
programme.desc: The actor joins forces with his brother David and his uncle Matheson to try out landscape painting in the fresh air on the hunt for ways to relax that do not cost a fortune. programme.title: Expedition with Steve Backshall

programme.title: Expedition with Steve Backshall

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The Nazi occupation of Holland, which began relatively peacefully with Dutch civil servants continuing to run their own country, but eventually resulted in many thousands of deaths through the attempted elimination of Jewish citizens and starvation of the non-Jewish inhabitants. programme.title: Warbird Workshop

programme.title: Warbird Workshop
programme.desc: On a wartime bomber base in Lincolnshire, veteran engineer Gerry Cooper rebuilds two pre-war biplanes with glorious histories; the US Navy N3N and the Morane Saulnier 315

programme.title: David Jason's Great British Inventions
programme.desc: The actor examines British innovations in the history of engineering. programme.title: Robson Green's Weekend Escapes
programme.desc: The actor joins forces with his brother David and his uncle Matheson to try out landscape painting in the fresh air on the hunt for ways to relax that do not cost a fortune. programme.title: Expedition with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: Steve and an elite team of rock climbers head to the desert of southern Oman to attempt the first ascent of a towering escarpment, hoping to discover the rare and elusive Arabian leopard

programme.title: Expedition with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: Steve and the team battle through the jungle into the heart of Indonesian Borneo. They are on their way to meet experts who have discovered ancient cave art thought to be among the oldest on the planet. But they have also heard rumours that the mountain range contains further unmapped caves - and Steve wants to be the first to track them down, and whatever is hidden within

programme.title: Expedition with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: Steve is on a mission to explore the Arctic at the most volatile and dangerous time of the year - the spring melt. Paddling through ever-shifting sea ice and kayaking the world's largest fjord, he and his team are on constant alert for giant icebergs and hungry polar bears

programme.title: Expedition with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: Steve travels deep into Yucatán, Mexico, on the trail of the ancient Maya. His team, including Mexican archaeologist Guillermo de Andaa, pioneering cave diver Robbie Schmittner and former Royal Marine Aldo Kane, want to explore secret caves known as 'the place of fear'

programme.title: Trawlermen
programme.desc: Prawn boat Amity gets caught in a winter storm 100 miles from shore, and is in danger of sinking while its nets are stuck on the seabed. programme.title: Trawlermen

programme.title: Trawlermen

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Laurence Olivier narrates the story of the D-Day landings in June 1944, when the Allies launched the biggest ever amphibious invasion on the beaches of Normandy. Although both sides suffered heavy casualties, it proved to be the turning point in the war against Germany

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The Nazi occupation of Holland, which began relatively peacefully with Dutch civil servants continuing to run their own country, but eventually resulted in many thousands of deaths through the attempted elimination of Jewish citizens and starvation of the non-Jewish inhabitants. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Laurence Olivier narrates an account of the liberation of Paris and Brussels in 1944, events which to many signified the end of the war. However, as the German forces retreated, Poland fell victim to one of the most tragic events of the war, Documentary chronicling the Second World War

programme.title: The World at War

programme.title: Harry Enfield and Chums
programme.desc: More expertly crafted comedy sketches from Harry Enfield and colleagues-in-mirth Paul Whitehouse and Kathy Burke, featuring the Camp Jockeys, the Scousers and Kevin the teenager

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Mary walks Julian through her journey of self-discovery and how she finally found her voice. Thomas realises enough is enough - he must go 'cold turkey' on his Alison addiction and needs Pat's undivided attention. Old friends of Mike check in to the guesthouse and agree to share the surprising secret of their success. The Captain is thrown by a change to his daily routine, so Kitty steps in to teach him how to live in the moment. Robin helps Lady Button to remember lost friends

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: The spooks' thoughtless behaviour sends Alison into meltdown, prompting them to come up with a creative way to get back into her good books and show her how truly sorry they are. Mary however, believing she has done nothing wrong, refuses to take any responsibility, not after the fiery fate that befell her the last time she took the blame for something she didn't do. Alison and Mike have a last-minute cancellation for the guesthouse and decide that a relaxing day off is exactly what they need. Comedy, starring Charlotte Ritchie, Kiell Smith-Bynoe and Simon Farnaby

After a misunderstanding, Mrs Peacock finds her husband without his pants, which are in the hands of Mr Rumbold's secretary. Vintage comedy, with Mollie Sugden, Frank Thornton and Nicholas Smith

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.sub-title: Why Does Norman Clegg Buy Ladies' Elastic Stockings?
programme.desc: Howard asks a reluctant Clegg to deliver a message to Marina at her place of employment. Meanwhile, Seymour goes in search of oil, but manages to drill through a water main. Comedy, starring Peter Sallis and Michael Aldridge

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Seymour takes it upon himself to supervise repairs to a boat, and together with Clegg tries to persuade Compo to take up canoeing to improve his physique. Comedy, starring Michael Aldridge, Peter Sallis and Bill Owen

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: Fletcher takes on the role of agony aunt and ends up writing letters home for his fellow inmates - but he soon discovers domestic problems of his own when his daughter brings bad news on visiting day. Classic comedy, starring Ronnie Barker and Patricia Brake

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth continues her search for a small country retreat - but her idea of small is not quite the same as everybody else's. Comedy, starring Patricia Routledge and Clive Swift

programme.title: The Green Green Grass
programme.desc: Boycie senses an opportunity to get himself knighted when Marlene becomes heavily involved in charity work. Enlisting the help of their staff, Marlene tries to make it happen using her contacts, but achieving her husband's dream turns out to be harder than expected. Comedy, starring John Challis

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: Jones buys a load of black-market pigeons from wheeler-dealer Walker - but is horrified to discover the unfortunate birds were taken from Trafalgar Square. Classic comedy, starring Clive Dunn and James Beck. First broadcast in October 1972

programme.title: Ever Decreasing Circles
programme.desc: Martin looks forward to his annual Austrian holiday with Ann, Howard and Hilda. Comedy, starring Richard Briers and Penelope Wilton. First broadcast February 19, 1984

programme.title: Dad's Army: The Animations
programme.desc: The platoon arrest a supposed Nazi agent on charges of signalling to enemy aircraft

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Mavis goes to a wedding, but Gastric warns Granville of the danger of leaving her alone in a romantic setting. Eric, Cyril and Mr Newbold consider exploring their feminine side, while Mrs Featherstone is concerned that Mrs Rossi is becoming a bad influence on the shop, and Kath is on the receiving end of a new technique for matching customers to the perfect product

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Granville has to deal with a malfunctioning till, while Eric and Cyril decide it is time to show some macho spirit in the home of the Tour de Yorkshire and get back in the saddle. Meanwhile, Leroy tells Beth about his sneaky plan to find a blind date for Ruby, and Mr Newbold is sent on a mission by Mrs Featherstone to find out more about Mrs Rossi and whether she poses a threat to the local shop. But does he discover more than he bargained for?

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee is unhappy with his new living arrangements after Lucy's father separates from his wife and moves into the flat, so he takes on the role of counsellor in a bid to patch up the marriage and get his room back. Comedy, guest starring Geoffrey Whitehead, with Lee Mack and Sally Bretton

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lucy works on a government health initiative encouraging men to regularly check themselves for lumps, so an eager Lee follows the campaign's advice - only to discover something unexpected. Comedy, starring Sally Bretton, Lee Mack, Tim Vine and Katy Wix

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Mavis goes to a wedding, but Gastric warns Granville of the danger of leaving her alone in a romantic setting. Eric, Cyril and Mr Newbold consider exploring their feminine side, while Mrs Featherstone is concerned that Mrs Rossi is becoming a bad influence on the shop, and Kath is on the receiving end of a new technique for matching customers to the perfect product

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: The shopkeeping comedy returns with a festive instalment. Granville and Gastric find themselves playing marriage counsellors, while Leroy adopts a festive disguise to avoid relationship trouble of a different kind. Meanwhile, Mrs Featherstone struggles to get into the Christmas spirit and Kath attempts to bring the street together as one harmonious choir of carollers to beat Finkle Street once and for all - but does Granville have a trick up his sleeve to help them out? Starring David Jason, Tim Healy, James Baxter and Stephanie Cole

programme.title: Harry Enfield and Chums
programme.desc: Comedy sketches with the funnyman and partners in mirth Paul Whitehouse and Kathy Burke. Featuring characters including Kevin and Perry, Tim Nice-But-Dim and the Self Righteous Brothers

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee is unhappy with his new living arrangements after Lucy's father separates from his wife and moves into the flat, so he takes on the role of counsellor in a bid to patch up the marriage and get his room back. Comedy, guest starring Geoffrey Whitehead, with Lee Mack and Sally Bretton

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lucy works on a government health initiative encouraging men to regularly check themselves for lumps, so an eager Lee follows the campaign's advice - only to discover something unexpected. Comedy, starring Sally Bretton, Lee Mack, Tim Vine and Katy Wix

programme.title: Harry Enfield and Chums
programme.desc: More expertly crafted comedy sketches from Harry Enfield and colleagues-in-mirth Paul Whitehouse and Kathy Burke, featuring the Camp Jockeys, the Scousers and Kevin the teenager

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: A shocking revelation throws the Ghosts into disarray. Kitty looks to Pat for moral support, and while he is more than happy to oblige, he finds himself in deep water trying to offer her words of comfort. Another bombshell drops when Mike realises there have been some crossed wires over an event booking and he and Alison are forced into hosting an impromptu - and ill-prepared - children's birthday bash in order not to let two little girls down. Supernatural comedy, starring Charlotte Ritchie, Kiell Smith-Bynoe and Simon Farnaby

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Alison and Mike discover their neighbour has been scheming behind their backs - his outlandish tactics threatening the success of their guesthouse business. For Alison, this warrants all out war and she sets about playing him at his own game. If she is going to stoop to such trickery however, she'll need the help of one of the ghosts. Mike attempts to mix things up for future guests at the gatehouse by secretly investing in some not-so-cheap entertainment that Alison will definitely not approve of. Meanwhile, the Captain makes a discovery, leading the other ghosts to find a surprising new friend

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee, Lucy and Daisy go on a skiing trip to Eastern Europe, but as usual disaster isn't far behind. The trio of friends find themselves stuck in a cable car with a handful of other nervous tourists - one of whom just happens to be pregnant. Comedy, starring Lee Mack, Sally Bretton and Katy Wix

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: When a noisy neighbour moves in, Lucy complains neither Tim nor Lee is man enough to get him to quieten down. The friends take it to heart and try to prove their masculinity, one by joining a gym and the other by confronting the new arrival - only to find he is a creepy psychopath with the eyes of a killer

programme.title: Harry Enfield and Chums
programme.desc: More expertly crafted comedy sketches from Harry Enfield and colleagues-in-mirth Paul Whitehouse and Kathy Burke, featuring the Camp Jockeys, the Scousers and Kevin the teenager

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Mary walks Julian through her journey of self-discovery and how she finally found her voice. Thomas realises enough is enough - he must go 'cold turkey' on his Alison addiction and needs Pat's undivided attention. Old friends of Mike check in to the guesthouse and agree to share the surprising secret of their success. The Captain is thrown by a change to his daily routine, so Kitty steps in to teach him how to live in the moment. Robin helps Lady Button to remember lost friends

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: The spooks' thoughtless behaviour sends Alison into meltdown, prompting them to come up with a creative way to get back into her good books and show her how truly sorry they are. Mary however, believing she has done nothing wrong, refuses to take any responsibility, not after the fiery fate that befell her the last time she took the blame for something she didn't do. Alison and Mike have a last-minute cancellation for the guesthouse and decide that a relaxing day off is exactly what they need. Comedy, starring Charlotte Ritchie, Kiell Smith-Bynoe and Simon Farnaby

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: The Green Green Grass
programme.desc: Boycie discovers Marlene has been discussing their marital problems with a counsellor, and the subsequent row results in him being thrown out of the house. With the hotel booked up and his car in a pond, Boycie's only option is to sleep on Bryan's caravan floor. Comedy, starring John Challis and Sue Holderness

programme.title: Ever Decreasing Circles
programme.desc: Martin's suspicions about Paul's bad influence seem to be confirmed when he spots a half-naked woman in next door's back garden. Comedy, starring Richard Briers. First broadcast February 12, 1984

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: Fletch desperately wants to get out of the tedious task of making fishing nets, and hatches a crafty scheme to get himself assigned to the library. Classic comedy, starring Ronnie Barker

programme.title: The Green Green Grass
programme.desc: Boycie discovers a medieval recipe for traditional liquor among some ancient scrolls and goes into full-scale production, building a distillery in his barn and buying up all the potatoes he can find. The business goes well, but his success attracts some unwelcome visitors - the Driscoll brothers. John Challis and Sue Holderness star

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.sub-title: The Erotic Dreams of Mrs Slocombe
programme.desc: In the unlikeliest love match of all time, Mrs Slocombe woos confirmed bachelor Mr Humphries in a bid to make all her romantic dreams come true. Mollie Sugden and John Inman star

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.sub-title: The Woollen Mills of Your Mind
programme.desc: Compo takes up marathon running, hoping that it will persuade Nora Batty to fall into his arms. Long-running rural comedy, starring Bill Owen and Brian Wilde

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Danny Dyer and daughter Dani join Natasha Raskin Sharp and Philip Serrell on an antiques hunt in Essex that features pirates, a raft race, sunken treasure and a Chinese banquet

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Countdown's Rachel Riley and her husband and former Strictly partner Pasha Kovalev compete, teaming up with Paul Martin and David Harper to help scour the shops of Essex. Rachel gets to grips with a longbow and discovers why English archers were so formidable in war during the Middle Ages, while Pasha's weapon of choice makes a bigger bang as he finds out how the town of Colchester was besieged during the English Civil War

programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous
programme.desc: Edina decides to soak away her worries in a flotation tank and is amazed at its life-changing effects on both herself and those around her - so amazed that she agrees to adopt a Romanian orphan and even attends an open day at Saffron's school. Award-winning comedy, starring Jennifer Saunders, Joanna Lumley and Julia Sawalha

programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous
programme.desc: Edina takes time out from being PR guru to the country's biggest stars to become involved with Patsy's fashion magazine. Cosmo and Vogue beware - two new lushes are in town. Comedy, starring Jennifer Saunders and Joanna Lumley, with guest appearances by Dawn French and Adrian Edmondson

programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous
programme.desc: Edina hits 40 and goes into a panic at the realisation that she now officially has one foot in the grave - so Saffy decides to cheer her up by throwing a party. Unfortunately, the guests fail to get along, and it all ends in tears. Hit comedy, starring Jennifer Saunders, Joanna Lumley, Julia Sawalha and June Whitfield

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: MasterChef Australia
programme.desc: The cooks are divided into green and blue teams for the first team service challenge, with two and a half hours to serve entrees and showcase an ingredient from the judges' table

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: DIY SOS

programme.title: Tipping Point

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Feisty feline Driver has already survived trauma during his seven years of life, when he lost an eye as the result of an accident. His indomitable spirit is about to be severely tested again, as he's rushed into Fitzpatrick's as an emergency, suffering from a hip injury and serious trauma having been hit by a car. Seven-year-old Dobermann Dexter has serious spinal problems, collapsing on his back legs and tumbling on his front legs. His owners meet Noel to discuss options for his treatment

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Scans reveal multiple sites of deformity affecting Milo the Dalmatian's vertebrae, which are squashing both his spinal cord and the nerves coming out on either side. Noel offers the option of distraction fusion surgery to relieve both the pressure and the pain, while Laura and Max bring in their 10-month-old Chuskie pup Moon, who despite his youth has his life blighted by severe developmental elbow disease

programme.title: Shades of Blue
programme.desc: Harlee struggles under the pressure of acting as an informant on her crew - when Wozniak worries about a mole, she suggests putting everyone through a lie detector test

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Comedian Dom Joly and actress Pauline McLynn are in Lancashire with James Braxton and Philip Serrell, buying antique bamboo and teapots. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Guest designer Charlie Luxton joins Nick Knowles and the team in Stoke-on-Trent, where they meet terminally ill Haydn Spice and his family. Nick rallies friends, relatives and local traders to turn their property, which resembles a building site, into a comfortable home

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team head to Cirencester, Gloucestershire, to help the Beasleys finish work on an incomplete extension to their former council house. The project has taken a back seat to ill health and loss of work over the previous five years, leaving the family on the brink of despair, so local tradespeople, members of the community and designer Charlie Luxton are enlisted to help with the build

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Years of bad luck and illness have left the Wrights unable to finish converting their bungalow in Ottery St Mary, east Devon, into a four-bedroom family home. With this in mind, Nick Knowles enlists the aid of local tradespeople and guest designer Charlie Luxton to help them complete the project

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Comedian Dom Joly and actress Pauline McLynn are in Lancashire with James Braxton and Philip Serrell, buying antique bamboo and teapots. With four hundred pounds and two sporty classic cars, the teams test their antiques knowledge in a quest to bring home the big bucks at an auction in Cheshire

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Actors Robert Daws and Mark Curry go antique hunting in Lancashire, where they find a real-life Sherlock Holmes and join a drag queen cabaret. Roo Irvine and Izzy Balmer offer expert advice

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel treats nine-month-old cockapoo Betsy, whose owners Sophie and Dan are concerned about a dragging front paw caused by nerve damage, to see if there are surgical options. But when Betsy develops gangrene, she has to have her paw amputated, and in order to salvage the rest of her limb, Noel must replace her amputated foot with a skeletally-anchored implant called a PerFiTS (Percutaneous Fixation to the Skeleton)

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Documentary featuring one of the most emotional and heart-rending stories ever filmed - that of Noel Fitzpatrick's own dog, Keira. In September 2020, Noel's life was turned upside down when the 13-year-old border terrier was hit by a car right outside the practice. Having sustained a ruptured abdominal lining and urinary bladder, a crushed sacrum and pelvis and a dislocated hip, Noel's colleagues worked tirelessly to repair extensive internal damage. Suddenly, the surgeon renowned for finding solutions for even the most challenging injuries, had to steel himself for the most important surgery of his life

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Guest designer Charlie Luxton joins Nick Knowles and the team in Stoke-on-Trent, where they meet terminally ill Haydn Spice and his family. Nick rallies friends, relatives and local traders to turn their property, which resembles a building site, into a comfortable home

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team head to Cirencester, Gloucestershire, to help the Beasleys finish work on an incomplete extension to their former council house. The project has taken a back seat to ill health and loss of work over the previous five years, leaving the family on the brink of despair, so local tradespeople, members of the community and designer Charlie Luxton are enlisted to help with the build

programme.title: Pete Wicks: For Dogs' Sake
programme.desc: Strictly Come Dancing and TOWIE star Pete Wicks immerses himself in the heart-warming and emotional world of dog care at Basildon's Dogs Trust rehoming centre. It's Pete's first day at Dogs Trust, and he meets a pug facing a worrying surgery and pregnant dog who was seized at the border

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team head to Chesterfield, Derbyshire, to help Lisa and Richard Watson. With an expanding family and a young daughter who requires 24-hour care, builder Richard has not been able to complete an extension he started, but along with support from the building trade and local community, the team hopes to lift the strain on the family

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team travel to a village in Conwy, north Wales, to help a widower finish building his home. Since the death of his wife and being diagnosed with cancer, David Williams has been unable to work on the property and lives in a caravan with his young daughter Erica - so the crew helps to make the house habitable with the support of the local community and traders

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Mike makes a discovery among some old relics in the garden, which leads Thomas to go down memory lane with Alison and share a story from his past that he has never told before. Meanwhile, Lady Button, Pat and the Captain have gathered to hear Julian give one of his scheduled talks, but curiosity sends them upstairs and they can't resist getting involved with Thomas's tale as well

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: When Mike spends his first night alone in the house with the ghosts, he assures Alison she should go out and let her hair down. The ghosts happily pass the time with a music club, but they are interrupted by some uninvited guests and it becomes their mission to try to alert Mike and figure out how to protect the house from the intruders.

With an expanding family and a young daughter who requires 24-hour care, builder Richard has not been able to complete an extension he started, but along with support from the building trade and local community, the team hopes to lift the strain on the family

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team travel to a village in Conwy, north Wales, to help a widower finish building his home. Since the death of his wife and being diagnosed with cancer, David Williams has been unable to work on the property and lives in a caravan with his young daughter Erica - so the crew helps to make the house habitable with the support of the local community and traders

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Comedian Dom Joly and actress Pauline McLynn are in Lancashire with James Braxton and Philip Serrell, buying antique bamboo and teapots. With four hundred pounds and two sporty classic cars, the teams test their antiques knowledge in a quest to bring home the big bucks at an auction in Cheshire

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Heavy snow disrupts plans for the wedding as travel to Button House becomes near impossible, so Alison and Mike have to rally round to make sure Sam and Clare's big day goes ahead. The Captain is in his element with the emergency preparations to the house, but Pat is distracted by an unexpected reminder of his past, while Lady Button is aghast at what Alison is allowing to take place at her former home

programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous
programme.desc: Childhood pals Edina and Patsy have a bitter bust-up and set out to find new best friends. Acclaimed comedy, starring Jennifer Saunders and Joanna Lumley, with guest appearances by Britt Ekland, Lulu and Zandra Rhodes

programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous
programme.desc: Edina's ex-husband Marshall threatens to cut off her alimony payments, throwing her into a panic over how she is going to survive without regular deliveries of groceries from Harrods. Comedy, starring Jennifer Saunders and Joanna Lumley

programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous
programme.desc: Patsy and Edina have a rebirthing experience in the living room after setting the kitchen alight. Comedy, starring Jennifer Saunders and Joanne Lumley

programme.desc: Paul is picking up a stunning 1961 Vauxhall Cresta, inspired by American design, the car changed the face of British motoring

programme.desc: The Mathewsons sell a 1958 Bedford D122 and a 1969 Vanden Plas Princess. They also meet a collector who may be willing to finally part with his 220-car collection

programme.title: Shipwreck Secrets
programme.desc: A marine biologist and a deep-sea diver explore Chuuk Lagoon, one of the world's greatest underwater graveyards, for a wreck discovered by sonar nearly 20 years ago

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Dave's son Jack heads to Cheshire to collect two cars with a 35-year age difference, and luckily for Mathewsons, Lucy knows exactly what's been brought in for auction

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: A 1970 Excalibur Roadstar, once owned by Roy Orbison, arrives at Thornton-le-Dale and Derek heads to County Durham to collect a 1935 Austin 7 Ulster

programme.title: D-Day to Berlin
programme.desc: Sean Bean narrates a three-part docu-drama charting the campaign waged by Allied forces toward the end of the Second World War which culminated in the final conquest of Hitler's Reich. He begins with an account of the decisive struggle to break out from the Normandy beach-head in June 1944 and advance across Europe, drawing on the memories of those who fought on both sides, and charts the growing tension between American and British commanders Dwight D Eisenhower and General Montgomery

programme.title: D-Day to Berlin
programme.desc: Sean Bean narrates the story of the behind-the-scenes battles between Dwight D Eisenhower and Field Marshal Montgomery toward the end of 1944. He describes how Anglo-American relations came under threat as the Allies fought in the conference chamber for the right to direct the final assault on Germany. Veterans from both sides describe the struggle to capture the bridge over the Rhine at Arnhem and the terrible winter conflicts during the Battle of the Bulge

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team visit Somerleyton Hall in Suffolk, where items for appraisal include a rare glass vase whose owner had thought was fake, objects recovered from the plane that brought Rudolf Hess to Britain during the Second World War, and a fedora worn by Michael Jackson during a UK tour

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team examine objects brought to Hopetoun House by the Firth of Forth, close to Edinburgh. Among the items assessed are a rare illustration of Queen Victoria's first visit to Balmoral, a valuable pottery pig usually kept in a cat basket, and records from the early history of British broadcasting

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Derek is picking up two expertly crafted batmobile replicas in the home counties and Dave can't control his enthusiasm for a Ford US wartime jeep

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Paul is picking up a stunning 1961 Vauxhall Cresta, inspired by American design, the car changed the face of British motoring

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming sets out to explore the meandering waterways of inner city Birmingham including the Soho Loop and the Engine Arm Canal

programme.title: Train Truckers
programme.desc: A cherished wartime iron workhorse needs to be hauled 160 miles from the Churnet Valley Railway in Staffordshire to a winter extravaganza in Oxfordshire

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a look at life on each of the seven continents, revealing how each distinct land mass has shaped the creatures found there and the many challenges faced by animals in a modern world dominated by humans. He begins with Antarctica, where Weddell seals grind back the ice with their teeth, colourful starfish carpet the seabed, huge colonies of king penguins crowd any ice-free land, and four-ton elephant seals fight for territory on the beach. However, the ocean here is warming - and with that comes an uncertain future

programme.desc: Derek is picking up two expertly crafted batmobile replicas in the home counties and Dave can't control his enthusiasm for a Ford US wartime jeep
